units.text: Enter the KING, Lord John of LANCASTER, Earl of WESTMORELAND, with others

units.speaker: KING
units.text:
So shaken as we are, so wan with care,
Find we a time for frighted peace to pant
And breathe short-winded accents of new broils
To be commenced in strands afar remote.
No more the thirsty entrance of this soil
Shall daub her lips with her own children’s blood.
Nor more shall trenching war channel her fields,
Nor bruise her flow’rets with the armed hoofs
Of hostile paces. Those opposèd eyes,
Which, like the meteors of a troubled heaven,
All of one nature, of one substance bred,
Did lately meet in the intestine shock
And furious close of civil butchery
Shall now, in mutual well-beseeming ranks,
March all one way and be no more opposed
Against acquaintance, kindred, and allies.
The edge of war, like an ill-sheathèd knife,
No more shall cut his master. Therefore, friends,
As far as to the sepulcher of Christ—
Whose soldier now, under whose blessed cross
We are impressèd and engaged to fight—
Forthwith a power of English shall we levy,
Whose arms were molded in their mothers' womb
To chase these pagans in those holy fields
Over whose acres walked those blessèd feet
Which fourteen hundred years ago were nailed
For our advantage on the bitter cross.
But this our purpose now is twelve month old,
And bootless ’tis to tell you we will go.
Therefor we meet not now. Then let me hear
Of you, my gentle cousin Westmoreland,
What yesternight our council did decree
In forwarding this dear expedience.

units.speaker: WESTMORELAND
units.text:
My liege, this haste was hot in question,
And many limits of the charge set down
But yesternight: when all athwart there came
A post from Wales loaden with heavy news,
Whose worst was that the noble Mortimer,
Leading the men of Herefordshire to fight
Against the irregular and wild Glendower,
Was by the rude hands of that Welshman taken,
A thousand of his people butcherèd,
Upon whose dead corpse there was such misuse,
Such beastly shameless transformation
By those Welshwomen done, as may not be
Without much shame retold or spoken of.

units.speaker: KING
units.text:
It seems then that the tidings of this broil
Brake off our business for the Holy Land.

units.speaker: WESTMORELAND
units.text:
This matched with other did, my gracious lord.
For more uneven and unwelcome news
Came from the north and thus it did import:
On Holy-rood Day, the gallant Hotspur there,
Young Harry Percy, and brave Archibald,
That ever valiant and approvèd Scot,
At Holmedon met, where they did spend
A sad and bloody hour—
As by discharge of their artillery
And shape of likelihood the news was told;
For he that brought them, in the very heat
And pride of their contention did take horse,
Uncertain of the issue any way.

units.speaker: KING
units.text:
Here is a dear, a true-industrious friend,
Sir Walter Blunt, new lighted from his horse.
Stained with the variation of each soil
Betwixt that Holmedon and this seat of ours,
And he hath brought us smooth and welcome news.
The Earl of Douglas is discomfited;
Ten thousand bold Scots, two-and-twenty knights,
Balked in their own blood, did Sir Walter see
On Holmedon’s plains . Of prisoners Hotspur took
Mordake, Earl of Fife, and eldest son
To beaten Douglas, and the Earl of Atholl,
Of Murray, Angus, and Menteith.
And is not this an honorable spoil?
A gallant prize? Ha, cousin, is it not?

units.speaker: WESTMORELAND
units.text: In faith, it is a conquest for a prince to boast of.

units.speaker: KING
units.text:
Yea, there thou mak’st me sad, and mak’st me sin
In envy that my Lord Northumberland
Should be the father to so blest a son,
A son who is the theme of Honor’s tongue,
Amongst a grove the very straightest plant,
Who is sweet Fortune’s minion and her pride;
Whilst I, by looking on the praise of him,
See riot and dishonor stain the brow
Of my young Harry. O, that it could be proved
That some night-tripping fairy had exchanged
In cradle-clothes our children where they lay,
And called mine “Percy,” his “Plantagenet”!
Then would I have his Harry, and he mine.
But let him from my thoughts. What think you, coz,
Of this young Percy’s pride? The prisoners,
Which he in this adventure hath surprised
To his own use he keeps, and sends me word
I shall have none but Mordake, Earl of Fife.

units.speaker: WESTMORELAND
units.text:
This is his uncle’s teaching. This is Worcester,
Malevolent to you in all aspects,
Which makes him prune himself, and bristle up
The crest of youth against your dignity.

units.speaker: KING
units.text:
But I have sent for him to answer this.
And for this cause awhile we must neglect
Our holy purpose to Jerusalem.
Cousin, on Wednesday next our council we
Will hold at Windsor. So inform the lords.
But come yourself with speed to us again,
For more is to be said and to be done
Than out of anger can be utterèd.

units.speaker: WESTMORELAND
units.text: I will, my liege.
Exeunt
Enter HENRY, PRINCE of Wales, and Sir John FALSTAFF

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Now, Hal, what time of day is it, lad?

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Thou art so fat-witted with drinking of old sack, and
unbuttoning thee after supper, and sleeping upon benches
after noon, that thou hast forgotten to demand that
truly which thou wouldst truly know. What a devil hast
thou to do with the time of the day? Unless hours were
cups of sack, and minutes capons, and clocks the tongues
of bawds, and dials the signs of leaping-houses, and
the blessed sun himself a fair hot wench in
flame-colored taffeta, I see no reason why thou shouldst
be so superfluous to demand the time of the day.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Indeed, you come near me now, Hal, f or we that take
purses go by the moon and the seven stars, and not by
Phoebus, he, that wand'ring knight so fair. And I
prithee, sweet wag, when thou art king, as God save thy
Grace—Majesty, I should say, for grace thou wilt have
none—

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: What, none?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
No, by my troth, not so much as will serve to be
prologue to an egg and butter.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Well, how then? Come, roundly, roundly.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Marry, then, sweet wag, when thou art king, let not us
that are squires of the night’s body be called thieves
of the day’s beauty. Let us be Diana’s foresters,
gentlemen of the shade, minions of the moon, and let men
say we be men of good government, being governed, as
the sea is, by our noble and chaste mistress the moon,
under whose countenance we steal.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Thou sayest well, and it holds well too, for the
fortune of us that are the moon’s men doth ebb and flow
like the sea, being governed, as the sea is, by the
moon. As for proof now: a purse of gold most resolutely
snatched on Monday night and most dissolutely spent on
Tuesday morning, got with swearing “Lay by” and spent
with crying “Bring in”; now in as low an ebb as the foot
of the ladder, and by and by in as high a flow as the
ridge of the gallows.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
By the Lord, thou sayest true, lad. And is not my
hostess of the tavern a most sweet wench?

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
As the honey of Hybla, my old lad of the castle. And is
not a buff jerkin a most sweet robe of durance?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
How now, how now, mad wag? What, in thy quips and thy
quiddities? What a plague have I to do with a buff
jerkin?

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Why, what a pox have I to do with my hostess of the
tavern?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Well, thou hast called her to a reckoning many a time
and oft.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Did I ever call for thee to pay thy part?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: No, I’ll give thee thy due. Thou hast paid all there.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Yea, and elsewhere, so far as my coin would stretch,
and where it would not, I have used my credit.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Yea, and so used it that were it not here apparent that
thou art heir apparent— But I prithee, sweet wag, shall
there be gallows standing in England when thou art king?
And resolution thus fubbed as it is with the rusty curb
of old father Antic the law? Do not thou, when thou art
king, hang a thief.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: No, thou shalt.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Shall I? O rare! By the Lord, I’ll be a brave judge.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Thou judgest false already: I mean thou shalt have the
hanging of the thieves, and so become a rare hangman.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Well, Hal, well, and in some sort it jumps with my
humor as well as waiting in the court, I can tell you.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: For obtaining of suits?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Yea, for obtaining of suits, whereof the hangman hath
no lean wardrobe. 'Sblood, I am as melancholy as a gib
cat or a lugged bear.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Or an old lion, or a lover’s lute.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Yea, or the drone of a Lincolnshire bagpipe.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
What sayest thou to a hare, or the melancholy of
Moorditch?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Thou hast the most unsavory similes, and art indeed the
most comparative, rascaliest, sweet young prince. But,
Hal, I prithee trouble me no more with vanity. I would
to God thou and I knew where a commodity of good names
were to be bought. An old lord of the council rated me
the other day in the street about you, sir, but I marked
him not, and yet he talked very wisely, but I regarded
him not, and yet he talked wisely, and in the street,
too.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Thou didst well, for wisdom cries out in the streets
and no man regards it.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
O, thou hast damnable iteration, and art indeed able to
corrupt a saint. Thou hast done much harm upon me, Hal,
God forgive thee for it. Before I knew thee, Hal, I
knew nothing, and now am I, if a man should speak truly,
little better than one of the wicked. I must give over
this life, and I will give it over. By the Lord, an I do
not, I am a villain. I’ll be damned for never a king’s
son in Christendom.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Where shall we take a purse tomorrow, Jack?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Zounds, where thou wilt, lad. I’ll make one. An I do
not, call me villain and baffle me.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
I see a good amendment of life in thee, from praying to
purse-taking.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Why, Hal, ’tis my vocation, Hal. 'Tis no sin for a man
to labor in his vocation.
Enter POINS

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Poins!—Now shall we know if Gadshill have set a match.
O, if men were to be saved by merit, what hole in hell
were hot enough for him? This is the most omnipotent
villain that ever cried “Stand!” to a true man.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Good morrow, Ned.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text:
Good morrow, sweet Hal.—What says Monsieur Remorse?
What says Sir John Sack-and-Sugar? Jack, how agrees the
devil and thee about thy soul that thou soldest him on
Good Friday last for a cup of Madeira and a cold capon’s
leg?

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Sir John stands to his word. The devil shall have his
bargain, for he was never yet a breaker of proverbs. He
will give the devil his due.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text:
[To FALSTAFF] Then art thou damned for keeping thy word
with the devil.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Else he had been damned for cozening the devil.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text:
But, my lads, my lads, tomorrow morning, by four
o'clock, early at Gad’s Hill, there are pilgrims going
to Canterbury with rich offerings, and traders riding to
London with fat purses. I have vizards for you all. You
have horses for yourselves. Gadshill lies tonight in
Rochester. I have bespoke supper tomorrow night in
Eastcheap. We may do it as secure as sleep. If you will
go, I will stuff your purses full of crowns. If you will
not, tarry at home and be hanged.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Hear ye, Yedward, if I tarry at home and go not, I’ll
hang you for going.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: You will, chops?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Hal, wilt thou make one?

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Who, I rob? I a thief? not I, by my faith.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
There’s neither honesty, manhood, nor good fellowship
in thee, nor thou cam’st not of the blood royal, if thou
darest not stand for ten shillings.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Well then, once in my days I’ll be a madcap.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Why, that’s well said.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Well, come what will, I’ll tarry at home.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: By the Lord, I’ll be a traitor then when thou art king.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: I care not.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text:
Sir John, I prithee, leave the Prince and me alone. I
will lay him down such reasons for this adventure that
he shall go.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Well, God give thee the spirit of persuasion, and him
the ears of profiting, that what thou speakest may move,
and what he hears may be believed, that the true prince
may, for recreation sake, prove a false thief, for the
poor abuses of the time want countenance. Farewell. You
shall find me in Eastcheap.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Farewell, thou latter spring. Farewell, All-hallown
summer.
Exit FALSTAFF

units.speaker: POINS
units.text:
Now, my good sweet honey lord, ride with us tomorrow. I
have a jest to execute that I cannot manage alone.
Falstaff, Peto, Bardolph, and Gadshill shall rob those
men that we have already waylaid. Yourself and I will
not be there. And when they have the booty, if you and I
do not rob them, cut this head off from my shoulders.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: How shall we part with them in setting forth?

units.speaker: POINS
units.text:
Why, we will set forth before or after them, and
appoint them a place of meeting, wherein it is at our
pleasure to fail; and then will they adventure upon the
exploit themselves, which they shall have no sooner
achieved but we’ll set upon them.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Yea, but ’tis like that they will know us by our
horses, by our habits, and by every other appointment to
be ourselves.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text:
Tut, our horses they shall not see; I’ll tie them in
the wood. Our vizards we will change after we leave
them. And, sirrah, I have cases of buckram for the
nonce, to immask our noted outward garments.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Yea, but I doubt they will be too hard for us.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text:
Well, for two of them, I know them to be as true-bred
cowards as ever turned back; and for the third, if he
fight longer than he sees reason, I’ll forswear arms.
The virtue of this jest will be the incomprehensible lies
that this same fat rogue will tell us when we meet at
supper: how thirty at least he fought with, what wards,
what blows, what extremities he endured; and in the
reproof of this lies the jest.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Well, I’ll go with thee. Provide us all things
necessary and meet me tomorrow night in Eastcheap. There
I’ll sup. Farewell.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: Farewell, my lord.
Exit POINS

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
I know you all, and will awhile uphold
The unyoked humor of your idleness.
Yet herein will I imitate the sun,
Who doth permit the base contagious clouds
To smother up his beauty from the world,
That, when he please again to be himself,
Being wanted, he may be more wondered at
By breaking through the foul and ugly mists
Of vapors that did seem to strangle him.
If all the year were playing holidays,
To sport would be as tedious as to work,
But when they seldom come, they wished for come,
And nothing pleaseth but rare accidents.
So when this loose behavior I throw off
And pay the debt I never promisèd,
By how much better than my word I am,
By so much shall I falsify men’s hopes;
And, like bright metal on a sullen ground,
My reformation, glitt'ring o'er my fault,
Shall show more goodly and attract more eyes
Than that which hath no foil to set it off.
I’ll so offend to make offense a skill,
Redeeming time when men think least I will.
Exit
Enter the KING, NORTHUMBERLAND, WORCESTER, HOTSPUR, Sir Walter BLUNT, with others

units.speaker: KING
units.text:
My blood hath been too cold and temperate,
Unapt to stir at these indignities,
And you have found me, for accordingly
You tread upon my patience. But be sure
I will from henceforth rather be myself,
Mighty and to be feared, than my condition,
Which hath been smooth as oil, soft as young down,
And therefore lost that title of respect
Which the proud soul ne'er pays but to the proud.

units.speaker: WORCESTER
units.text:
Our house, my sovereign liege, little deserves
The scourge of greatness to be used on it,
And that same greatness too which our own hands
Have holp to make so portly.

units.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
units.text: My lord—

units.speaker: KING
units.text:
Worcester, get thee gone; for I do see
Danger and disobedience in thine eye.
O sir, your presence is too bold and peremptory,
And majesty might never yet endure
The moody frontier of a servant brow.
You have good leave to leave us. When we need
Your use and counsel, we shall send for you.
Exit WORCESTER

units.speaker: KING
units.text: [To NORTHUMBERLAND] You were about to speak.

units.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
units.text:
Yea, my good lord.
Those prisoners in your Highness' name demanded,
Which Harry Percy here at Holmedon took,
Were, as he says, not with such strength denied
As is delivered to your Majesty:
Either envy, therefore, or misprison
Is guilty of this fault, and not my son.

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text:
My liege, I did deny no prisoners.
But I remember, when the fight was done,
When I was dry with rage and extreme toil,
Breathless and faint, leaning upon my sword,
Came there a certain lord, neat, and trimly dressed,
Fresh as a bridegroom, and his chin new reaped
Showed like a stubble land at harvest home.
He was perfumèd like a milliner,
And ’twixt his finger and his thumb he held
A pouncet box, which ever and anon
He gave his nose and took ’t away again,
Who therewith angry, when it next came there,
Took it in snuff; and still he smiled and talked.
And as the soldiers bore dead bodies by,
He called them untaught knaves, unmannerly,
To bring a slovenly unhandsome corse
Betwixt the wind and his nobility.
With many holiday and lady terms
He questioned me; amongst the rest demanded
My prisoners in your Majesty’s behalf.
I then, all smarting with my wounds being cold,
To be so pestered with a popinjay,
Out of my grief and my impatience
Answered neglectingly I know not what—
He should, or he should not; for he made me mad
To see him shine so brisk and smell so sweet
And talk so like a waiting-gentlewoman
Of guns, and drums, and wounds—God save the mark!—
And telling me the sovereignest thing on earth
Was parmacety for an inward bruise,
And that it was great pity, so it was,
This villanous saltpeter should be digged
Out of the bowels of the harmless earth,
Which many a good tall fellow had destroyed
So cowardly, and but for these vile guns
He would himself have been a soldier.
This bald unjointed chat of his, my lord,
I answered indirectly, as I said,
And I beseech you, let not his report
Come current for an accusation
Betwixt my love and your high Majesty.

units.speaker: BLUNT
units.text:
The circumstance considered, good my lord,
Whate'er Lord Harry Percy then had said
To such a person and in such a place,
At such a time, with all the rest retold,
May reasonably die and never rise
To do him wrong or any way impeach
What then he said, so he unsay it now.

units.speaker: KING
units.text:
Why, yet he doth deny his prisoners,
But with proviso and exception
That we at our own charge shall ransom straight
His brother-in-law, the foolish Mortimer,
Who, on my soul, hath willfully betrayed
The lives of those that he did lead to fight
Against that great magician, damned Glendower,
Whose daughter, as we hear, the Earl of March
Hath lately married. Shall our coffers then
Be emptied to redeem a traitor home?
Shall we buy treason and indent with fears
When they have lost and forfeited themselves?
No, on the barren mountains let him starve,
For I shall never hold that man my friend
Whose tongue shall ask me for one penny cost
To ransom home revolted Mortimer.

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text:
Revolted Mortimer!
He never did fall off, my sovereign liege,
But by the chance of war. To prove that true
Needs no more but one tongue for all those wounds,
Those mouthèd wounds, which valiantly he took
When on the gentle Severn’s sedgy bank
In single opposition hand to hand
He did confound the best part of an hour
In changing hardiment with great Glendower.
Three times they breathed, and three times did they
drink,
Upon agreement, of swift Severn’s flood,
Who then, affrighted with their bloody looks,
Ran fearfully among the trembling reeds
And hid his crisp head in the hollow bank,
Bloodstainèd with these valiant combatants.
Never did bare and rotten policy
Color her working with such deadly wounds,
Nor could the noble Mortimer
Receive so many, and all willingly.
Then let not him be slandered with revolt.

units.speaker: KING
units.text:
Thou dost belie him, Percy; thou dost belie him.
He never did encounter with Glendower.
I tell thee, he durst as well have met the devil alone
As Owen Glendower for an enemy.
Art thou not ashamed? But, sirrah, henceforth
Let me not hear you speak of Mortimer.
Send me your prisoners with the speediest means,
Or you shall hear in such a kind from me
As will displease you.— My lord Northumberland,
We license your departure with your son.—
Send us your prisoners, or you will hear of it.
Exit KING Henry, BLUNT, and train

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text:
An if the devil come and roar for them,
I will not send them. I will after straight
And tell him so, for I will ease my heart,
Albeit I make a hazard of my head.

units.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
units.text:
What, drunk with choler? stay and pause awhile.
Here comes your uncle.
Enter WORCESTER

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text:
Speak of Mortimer?
Zounds, I will speak of him, and let my soul
Want mercy if I do not join with him.
Yea, on his part I’ll empty all these veins
And shed my dear blood drop by drop in the dust,
But I will lift the downtrod Mortimer
As high in the air as this unthankful King,
As this ingrate and cankered Bolingbroke.

units.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
units.text:
[To WORCESTER] Brother, the King hath made your nephew
mad.

units.speaker: WORCESTER
units.text: Who struck this heat up after I was gone?

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text:
He will forsooth have all my prisoners,
And when I urged the ransom once again
Of my wife’s brother, then his cheek looked pale,
And on my face he turned an eye of death,
Trembling even at the name of Mortimer.

units.speaker: WORCESTER
units.text:
I cannot blame him. Was not he proclaimed
By Richard, that dead is, the next of blood?

units.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
units.text:
He was; I heard the proclamation.
And then it was when the unhappy King—
Whose wrongs in us God pardon!—did set forth
Upon his Irish expedition;
From whence he, intercepted, did return
To be deposed and shortly murderèd.

units.speaker: WORCESTER
units.text:
And for whose death we in the world’s wide mouth
Live scandalized and foully spoken of.

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text:
But soft, I pray you. Did King Richard then
Proclaim my brother Edmund Mortimer
Heir to the crown?

units.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
units.text: He did; myself did hear it.

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text:
Nay then, I cannot blame his cousin King
That wished him on the barren mountains starve.
But shall it be that you that set the crown
Upon the head of this forgetful man
And for his sake wear the detested blot
Of murderous subornation—shall it be
That you a world of curses undergo,
Being the agents or base second means,
The cords, the ladder, or the hangman rather?
O, pardon me that I descend so low
To show the line and the predicament
Wherein you range under this subtle King.
Shall it for shame be spoken in these days,
Or fill up chronicles in time to come,
That men of your nobility and power
Did gage them both in an unjust behalf
(As both of you, God pardon it, have done)
To put down Richard, that sweet lovely rose,
An plant this thorn, this canker, Bolingbroke?
And shall it in more shame be further spoken
That you are fooled, discarded, and shook off
By him for whom these shames you underwent?
No, yet time serves wherein you may redeem
Your banished honors and restore yourselves
Into the good thoughts of the world again,
Revenge the jeering and disdain’d contempt
Of this proud King, who studies day and night
To answer all the debt he owes to you
Even with the bloody payment of your deaths.
Therefore I say—

units.speaker: WORCESTER
units.text:
Peace, cousin, say no more.
And now I will unclasp a secret book,
And to your quick-conceiving discontents
I’ll read you matter deep and dangerous,
As full of peril and adventurous spirit
As to o'erwalk a current roaring loud
On the unsteadfast footing of a spear.

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text:
If he fall in, good night, or sink or swim!
Send danger from the east unto the west,
So honor cross it from the north to south,
And let them grapple: O, the blood more stirs
To rouse a lion than to start a hare!

units.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
units.text:
Imagination of some great exploit
Drives him beyond the bounds of patience.

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text:
By heaven, methinks it were an easy leap
To pluck bright honor from the pale-faced moon,
Or dive into the bottom of the deep,
Where fathom line could never touch the ground,
And pluck up drownèd honor by the locks,
So he that doth redeem her thence might wear
Without corrival all her dignities.
But out upon this half-faced fellowship!

units.speaker: WORCESTER
units.text:
[To NORTHUMBERLAND] He apprehends a world of figures
here,
But not the form of what he should attend.—
[To HOTSPUR] Good cousin, give me audience for a while.

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text: I cry you mercy.

units.speaker: WORCESTER
units.text:
Those same noble Scots
That are your prisoners—

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text:
I’ll keep them all.
By God, he shall not have a Scot of them.
No, if a Scot would save his soul, he shall not.
I’ll keep them, by this hand!

units.speaker: WORCESTER
units.text:
You start away
And lend no ear unto my purposes:
Those prisoners you shall keep—

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text:
Nay, I will. That’s flat!
He said he would not ransom Mortimer,
Forbad my tongue to speak of Mortimer.
But I will find him when he lies asleep,
And in his ear I’ll hollo “Mortimer.”
Nay,
I’ll have a starling shall be taught to speak
Nothing but “Mortimer,” and give it him
To keep his anger still in motion.

units.speaker: WORCESTER
units.text: Hear you, cousin, a word.

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text:
All studies here I solemnly defy,
Save how to gall and pinch this Bolingbroke.
And that same sword-and-buckler Prince of Wales—
But that I think his father loves him not
And would be glad he met with some mischance—
I would have him poisoned with a pot of ale.

units.speaker: WORCESTER
units.text:
Farewell, kinsman. I’ll talk to you
When you are better tempered to attend.

units.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
units.text:
[To HOTSPUR] Why, what a wasp-stung and impatient fool
Art thou to break into this woman’s mood,
Tying thine ear to no tongue but thine own!

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text:
Why, look you, I am whipped and scourged with rods,
Nettled and stung with pismires, when I hear
Of this vile politician, Bolingbroke.
In Richard’s time—what do you call the place?
A plague upon it! It is in Gloucestershire.
'Twas where the madcap duke his uncle kept,
His uncle York; where I first bowed my knee
Unto this king of smiles, this Bolingbroke.
'Sblood, when you and he came back from Ravenspurgh.

units.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
units.text: At Berkley Castle.

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text:
You say true.
Why, what a candy deal of courtesy
This fawning greyhound then did proffer me:
“Look when his infant fortune came to age,”
And “gentle Harry Percy,” and “kind cousin.”
O, the devil take such cozeners!— God forgive me!
Good uncle, tell your tale. I have done.

units.speaker: WORCESTER
units.text:
Nay, if you have not, to it again.
We will stay your leisure.

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text: I have done, i' faith.

units.speaker: WORCESTER
units.text:
Then once more to your Scottish prisoners:
Deliver them up without their ransom straight,
And make the Douglas' son your only mean
For powers in Scotland, which, for divers reasons
Which I shall send you written, be assured
Will easily be granted.— ( to NORTHUMBERLAND ) You, my
lord,
Your son in Scotland being thus employed,
Shall secretly into the bosom creep
Of that same noble prelate, well beloved,
The Archbishop.

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text: Of York, is it not?

units.speaker: WORCESTER
units.text:
True; who bears hard
His brother’s death at Bristol, the Lord Scroop.
I speak not this in estimation,
As what I think might be, but what I know
Is ruminated, plotted, and set down,
And only stays but to behold the face
Of that occasion that shall bring it on.

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text: I smell it. Upon my life, it will do well.

units.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
units.text: Before the game is afoot thou still let’st slip.

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text:
Why, it cannot choose but be a noble plot.
And then the power of Scotland and of York
To join with Mortimer, ha?

units.speaker: WORCESTER
units.text: And so they shall.

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text: In faith, it is exceedingly well aimed.

units.speaker: WORCESTER
units.text:
And ’tis no little reason bids us speed
To save our heads by raising of a head,
For, bear ourselves as even as we can,
The King will always think him in our debt,
And think we think ourselves unsatisfied,
Till he hath found a time to pay us home.
And see already how he doth begin
To make us strangers to his looks of love.

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text: He does, he does. We’ll be revenged on him.

units.speaker: WORCESTER
units.text:
Cousin, farewell. No further go in this
Than I by letters shall direct your course.
When time is ripe, which will be suddenly,
I’ll steal to Glendower and Lord Mortimer,
Where you and Douglas and our powers at once,
As I will fashion it, shall happily meet
To bear our fortunes in our own strong arms,
Which now we hold at much uncertainty.

units.speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
units.text: Farewell, good brother. We shall thrive, I trust.

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text:
Uncle, adieu: O, let the hours be short
Till fields and blows and groans applaud our sport.
Exeunt
Enter a CARRIER with a lantern in his hand

units.speaker: FIRST CARRIER
units.text:
Heigh-ho! An it be not four by the day, I’ll be hanged.
Charles’s Wain is over the new chimney, and yet our
horse not packed.—What, ostler!

units.speaker: OSTLER
units.text: [within] Anon, anon.

units.speaker: FIRST CARRIER
units.text:
I prithee, Tom, beat Cut’s saddle. Put a few flocks in
the point. Poor jade is wrung in the withers out of all
cess.
Enter another CARRIER

units.speaker: SECOND CARRIER
units.text:
Peas and beans are as dank here as a dog, and that is
the next way to give poor jades the bots. This house is
turned upside down since Robin ostler died.

units.speaker: FIRST CARRIER
units.text:
Poor fellow never joyed since the price of oats rose.
It was the death of him.

units.speaker: SECOND CARRIER
units.text:
I think this be the most villanous house in all London
road for fleas. I am stung like a tench.

units.speaker: FIRST CARRIER
units.text:
Like a tench? By the Mass, there is ne'er a king
christen could be better bit than I have been since the
first cock.

units.speaker: SECOND CARRIER
units.text:
Why, they will allow us ne'er a jordan, and then we
leak in your chimney, and your chamber-lye breeds fleas
like a loach.

units.speaker: FIRST CARRIER
units.text: What, ostler, come away and be hanged. Come away.

units.speaker: SECOND CARRIER
units.text:
I have a gammon of bacon and two races of ginger to be
delivered as far as Charing Cross.

units.speaker: FIRST CARRIER
units.text:
God’s body, the turkeys in my pannier are quite
starved.— What, ostler! A plague on thee! Hast thou
never an eye in thy head? Canst not hear? An ’twere not
as good deed as drink to break the pate on thee, I am a
very villain. Come, and be hanged. Hast no faith in
thee?
Enter GADSHILL

units.speaker: GADSHILL
units.text: Good morrow, carriers. What’s o'clock?

units.speaker: FIRST CARRIER
units.text: I think it be two o'clock.

units.speaker: GADSHILL
units.text:
I prithee, lend me thy lantern to see my gelding in the
stable.

units.speaker: FIRST CARRIER
units.text:
Nay, by God, soft. I know a trick worth two of that, i'
faith.

units.speaker: GADSHILL
units.text: [To SECOND CARRIER ] I pray thee, lend me thine.

units.speaker: SECOND CARRIER
units.text:
Ay, when, canst tell? “Lend me thy lantern,” quoth he.
Marry, I’ll see thee hanged first.

units.speaker: GADSHILL
units.text:
Sirrah carrier, what time do you mean to come to
London?

units.speaker: SECOND CARRIER
units.text:
Time enough to go to bed with a candle, I warrant thee.
Come, neighbour Mugs, we’ll call up the gentlemen. They
will along with company, for they have great charge.
Exeunt CARRIERS

units.speaker: GADSHILL
units.text: What ho, chamberlain!

units.speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
units.text: [within] At hand, quoth pickpurse.

units.speaker: GADSHILL
units.text:
That’s even as fair as “at hand, quoth the
Chamberlain,” for thou variest no more from picking of
purses than giving direction doth from laboring: thou
layest the plot how.
Enter CHAMBERLAIN

units.speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
units.text:
Good morrow, Master Gadshill. It holds current that I
told you yesternight: there’s a franklin in the Wild of
Kent hath brought three hundred marks with him in gold.
I heard him tell it to one of his company last night at
supper—a kind of auditor, one that hath abundance of
charge too, God knows what. They are up already and call
for eggs and butter. They will away presently.

units.speaker: GADSHILL
units.text:
Sirrah, if they meet not with Saint Nicholas' clerks,
I’ll give thee this neck.

units.speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
units.text:
No, I’ll none of it. I pray thee keep that for the
hangman, for I know thou worshipest Saint Nicholas as
truly as a man of falsehood may.

units.speaker: GADSHILL
units.text:
What talkest thou to me of the hangman? If I hang, I’ll
make a fat pair of gallows, for if I hang, old Sir John
hangs with me, and thou knowest he is no starveling.
Tut, there are other Troyans that thou dream’st not of,
the which for sport sake are content to do the
profession some grace, that would, if matters should be
looked into, for their own credit sake make all whole. I
am joined with no foot-land-rakers, no long-staff
sixpenny strikers, none of these mad mustachio
purple-hued malt-worms, but with nobility and
tranquillity, burgomasters and great oneyers, such as
can hold in, such as will strike sooner than speak, and
speak sooner than drink, and drink sooner than pray, and
yet, zounds, I lie, for they pray continually to their
saint the commonwealth, or rather not pray to her but
prey on her, for they ride up and down on her and make
her their boots.

units.speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
units.text:
What, the commonwealth their boots? Will she hold out
water in foul way?

units.speaker: GADSHILL
units.text:
She will, she will. Justice hath liquored her. We steal
as in a castle, cocksure. We have the receipt of fern
seed; we walk invisible.

units.speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
units.text:
Nay, by my faith, I think you are more beholding to the
night than to fern seed for your walking invisible.

units.speaker: GADSHILL
units.text:
Give me thy hand. Thou shalt have a share in our
purchase, as I am a true man.

units.speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
units.text: Nay, rather let me have it as you are a false thief.

units.speaker: GADSHILL
units.text:
Go to. Homo is a common name to all men. Bid the ostler
bring my gelding out of the stable. Farewell, you muddy
knave.
Exeunt
Enter PRINCE HENRY, POINS, BARDOLPH, and PETO

units.speaker: POINS
units.text:
Come, shelter, shelter! I have removed Falstaff’s
horse, and he frets like a gummed velvet.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Stand close.
Exit POINS, BARDOLPH, and PETO exit
Enter FALSTAFF

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Poins! Poins, and be hanged! Poins!

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Peace, you fat-kidneyed rascal. What a brawling dost
thou keep!

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Where’s Poins, Hal?

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
He is walked up to the top of the hill. I’ll go seek
him.
Exit PRINCE HENRY

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
I am accursed to rob in that thief’s company. The
rascal hath removed my horse and tied him I know not
where. If I travel but four foot by the square further
afoot, I shall break my wind. Well, I doubt not but to
die a fair death for all this, if I ’scape hanging for
killing that rogue. I have forsworn his company hourly
any time this two-and-twenty years, and yet I am
bewitched with the rogue’s company. If the rascal hath
not given me medicines to make me love him, I’ll be
hanged. It could not be else: I have drunk medicines.—
Poins! Hal! A plague upon you both.—Bardolph! Peto!— I’ll
starve ere I’ll rob a foot further. An ’twere not as
good a deed as drink to turn true man and to leave these
rogues, I am the veriest varlet that ever chewed with a
tooth. Eight yards of uneven ground is threescore and
ten miles afoot with me, and the stony-hearted villains
know it well enough. A plague upon it when thieves
cannot be true one to another!
Enter PRINCE HENRY, POINS, PETO, and BARDOLPH

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
A plague upon you all! Give me my horse, you rogues.
Give me my horse and be hanged!

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Peace, you fat guts! Lie down, lay thine ear close to
the ground, and list if thou canst hear the tread of
travelers.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Have you any levers to lift me up again being down?
'Sblood, I’ll not bear mine own flesh so far afoot again
for all the coin in thy father’s Exchequer. What a
plague mean you to colt me thus?

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Thou liest. Thou art not colted; thou art uncolted.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
I prithee, good Prince Hal, help me to my horse, good
king’s son.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Out, you rogue! Shall I be your ostler?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Hang thyself in thine own heir-apparent garters! If I
be ta'en, I’ll peach for this. An I have not ballads
made on you all and sung to filthy tunes, let a cup of
sack be my poison—when a jest is so forward, and afoot
too! I hate it.
Enter GADSHILL

units.speaker: GADSHILL
units.text: Stand.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: So I do, against my will.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text:
O, ’tis our setter. I know his voice, Bardolph. —What
news?

units.speaker: GADSHILL
units.text:
Case you, case you. On with your vizards. There’s money
of the King’s coming down the hill. 'Tis going to the
King’s Exchequer.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: You lie, you rogue. 'Tis going to the King’s Tavern.

units.speaker: GADSHILL
units.text: There’s enough to make us all.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: To be hanged.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Sirs, you four shall front them in the narrow lane. Ned
Poins and I will walk lower. If they ’scape from your
encounter, then they light on us.

units.speaker: PETO
units.text: How many be there of them?

units.speaker: GADSHILL
units.text: Some eight or ten.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Zounds, will they not rob us?

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: What, a coward, Sir John Paunch?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Indeed, I am not John of Gaunt, your grandfather, but
yet no coward, Hal.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Well, we leave that to the proof.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text:
Sirrah Jack, thy horse stands behind the hedge. When
thou needest him, there thou shalt find him. Farewell,
and stand fast.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Now cannot I strike him, if I should be hanged.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: [aside to POINS ] Ned, where are our disguises?

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: [aside to PRINCE HENRY ] Here, hard by. Stand close.
Exeunt PRINCE HENRY and POINS

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Now, my masters, happy man be his dole, say I. Every
man to his business.
Enter the TRAVELERS

units.speaker: FIRST TRAVELER
units.text:
Come, neighbor, the boy shall lead our horses down the
hill.
We’ll walk afoot awhile and ease our legs.

units.speaker: THIEVES
units.text: Stand!

units.speaker: TRAVELERS
units.text: Jesus bless us!

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Strike! Down with them! Cut the villains' throats! Ah,
whoreson caterpillars, bacon-fed knaves, they hate us
youth. Down with them! Fleece them!

units.speaker: TRAVELERS
units.text: O, we are undone, both we and ours forever!

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Hang, you gorbellied knaves! Are you undone? No, you
fat chuffs. I would your store were here. On, bacons,
on! What, you knaves, young men must live. You are
grandjurors, are you? We’ll jure you, faith.
Here they rob them and bind them. Exeunt
Enter PRINCE HENRY and POINS

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
The thieves have bound the true men. Now could thou and
I rob the thieves and go merrily to London, it would be
argument for a week, laughter for a month, and a good
jest forever.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: Stand close, I hear them coming.
PRINCE HENRY and POINS hide. Enter the thieves again

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Come, my masters, let us share, and then to horse
before day. An the Prince and Poins be not two arrant
cowards, there’s no equity stirring. There’s no more
valor in that Poins than in a wild duck.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Your money!

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: Villains!
They all run away, and FALSTAFF, after a blow or two, runs away too, leaving the booty behind them.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Got with much ease. Now merrily to horse.
The thieves are all scattered, and possessed with fear
So strongly that they dare not meet each other.
Each takes his fellow for an officer.
Away, good Ned. Falstaff sweats to death,
And lards the lean earth as he walks along.
Were ’t not for laughing, I should pity him.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: How the fat rogue roared!
Exeunt
Enter HOTSPUR alone, reading a letter

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text:
But, for mine own part, my lord, I could be well
contented to be there, in respect of the love I bear
your house. He could be contented; why is he not, then?
In respect of the love he bears our house—he shows in
this he loves his own barn better than he loves our
house. Let me see some more. The purpose you undertake
is dangerous. Why, that’s certain. 'Tis dangerous to
take a cold, to sleep, to drink; but I tell you, my lord
fool, out of this nettle, danger, we pluck this flower,
safety. The purpose you undertake is dangerous, the
friends you have named uncertain, the time itself
unsorted, and your whole plot too light for the
counterpoise of so great an opposition. Say you so, say
you so? I say unto you again, you are a shallow,
cowardly hind, and you lie. What a lack-brain is this!
By the Lord, our plot is a good plot as ever was laid,
our friends true and constant—a good plot, good friends,
and full of expectation; an excellent plot, very good
friends. What a frosty-spirited rogue is this! Why, my
Lord of York commends the plot and the general course of
the action. Zounds, an I were now by this rascal, I
could brain him with his lady’s fan. Is there not my
father, my uncle, and myself? Lord Edmund Mortimer, my
Lord of York, and Owen Glendower? Is there not besides
the Douglas? Have I not all their letters to meet me in
arms by the ninth of the next month, and are they not
some of them set forward already? What a pagan rascal is
this—an infidel! Ha, you shall see now in very
sincerity of fear and cold heart, will he to the King
and lay open all our proceedings. O, I could divide
myself and go to buffets, for moving such a dish of skim
milk with so honorable an action! Hang him, let him
tell the King. We are prepared. I will set forward
tonight.
Enter his lady, LADY PERCY

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text: How now, Kate? I must leave you within these two hours.

units.speaker: LADY PERCY
units.text:
O my good lord, why are you thus alone?
For what offense have I this fortnight been
A banished woman from my Harry’s bed?
Tell me, sweet lord, what is ’t that takes from thee
Thy stomach, pleasure, and thy golden sleep?
Why dost thou bend thine eyes upon the earth
And start so often when thou sit’st alone?
Why hast thou lost the fresh blood in thy cheeks
And given my treasures and my rights of thee
To thick-eyed musing and curst melancholy?
In thy faint slumbers I by thee have watched,
And heard thee murmur tales of iron wars,
Speak terms of manage to thy bounding steed,
Cry “Courage! To the field!” And thou hast talk’d
Of sallies and retires, of trenches, tents,
Of palisadoes, frontiers, parapets,
Of basilisks, of cannon, culverin,
Of prisoners' ransom and of soldiers slain,
And all the currents of a heady fight.
Thy spirit within thee hath been so at war,
And thus hath so bestirred thee in thy sleep,
That beads of sweat have stood upon thy brow
Like bubbles in a late-disturbèd stream,
And in thy face strange motions have appeared,
Such as we see when men restrain their breath
On some great sudden hest. O, what portents are these?
Some heavy business hath my lord in hand,
And I must know it, else he loves me not.

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text: What, ho!
Enter SERVANT

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text: Is Gilliams with the packet gone?

units.speaker: SERVANT
units.text: He is, my lord, an hour ago.

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text: Hath Butler brought those horses from the sheriff?

units.speaker: SERVANT
units.text: One horse, my lord, he brought even now.

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text: What horse? A roan, a crop-ear, is it not?

units.speaker: SERVANT
units.text: It is, my lord.

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text:
That roan shall be my throne.
Well, I will back him straight. O, Esperance!
Bid Butler lead him forth into the park.
Exit SERVANT

units.speaker: LADY PERCY
units.text: But hear you, my lord.

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text: What say’st thou, my lady?

units.speaker: LADY PERCY
units.text: What is it carries you away?

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text:
Why, my horse,
My love, my horse.

units.speaker: LADY PERCY
units.text:
Out, you mad-headed ape!
A weasel hath not such a deal of spleen
As you are tossed with. In faith,
I’ll know your business, Harry, that I will.
I fear my brother Mortimer doth stir
About his title, and hath sent for you
To line his enterprise; but if you go—

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text: —So far afoot, I shall be weary, love.

units.speaker: LADY PERCY
units.text:
Come, come, you paraquito, answer me
Directly unto this question that I ask.
In faith, I’ll break thy little finger, Harry,
An if thou wilt not tell me all things true.

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text:
Away!
Away, you trifler. Love, I love thee not.
I care not for thee, Kate. This is no world
To play with mammets and to tilt with lips.
We must have bloody noses and cracked crowns,
And pass them current too.—Gods me, my horse!—
What say’st thou, Kate? What would’st thou have with
me?

units.speaker: LADY PERCY
units.text:
Do you not love me? Do you not indeed?
Well, do not then, for since you love me not,
I will not love myself. Do you not love me?
Nay, tell me if you speak in jest or no.

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text:
Come, wilt thou see me ride?
And when I am a-horseback, I will swear
I love thee infinitely. But hark you, Kate,
I must not have you henceforth question me
Whither I go, nor reason whereabout.
Whither I must, I must; and to conclude,
This evening must I leave you, gentle Kate.
I know you wise, but yet no farther wise
Than Harry Percy’s wife; constant you are,
But yet a woman; and for secrecy
No lady closer, for I well believe
Thou wilt not utter what thou dost not know,
And so far will I trust thee, gentle Kate.

units.speaker: LADY PERCY
units.text: How? So far?

units.speaker: HOTSPUR
units.text:
Not an inch further. But hark you, Kate,
Whither I go, thither shall you go too.
Today will I set forth, tomorrow you.
Will this content you, Kate?

units.speaker: LADY PERCY
units.text: It must, of force.
Exeunt
Enter PRINCE HENRY and POINS

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Ned, prithee, come out of that fat room and lend me thy
hand to laugh a little.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: Where hast been, Hal?

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
With three or four loggerheads amongst three or
fourscore hogsheads. I have sounded the very bass string
of humility. Sirrah, I am sworn brother to a leash of
drawers, and can call them all by their christen names,
as Tom, Dick, and Francis. They take it already upon
their salvation that though I be but Prince of Wales,
yet I am the king of courtesy, and tell me flatly am no
proud jack, like Falstaff, but a Corinthian, a lad of
mettle, a good boy—by the Lord, so they call me— and when
I am King of England, I shall command all the good lads
in Eastcheap. They call drinking deep “dyeing scarlet,”
and when you breathe in your watering, they cry “Hem!”
and bid you “Play it off!” To conclude, I am so good a
proficient in one quarter of an hour that I can drink
with any tinker in his own language during my life. I
tell thee, Ned, thou hast lost much honor that thou wert
not with me in this action; but, sweet Ned—to sweeten
which name of Ned, I give thee this pennyworth of sugar,
clapped even now into my hand by an underskinker, one
that never spake other English in his life than “Eight
shillings and sixpence,” and “You are welcome,” with
this shrill addition, “Anon, anon, sir.—Score a pint of
bastard in the Half-moon,” or so. But, Ned, to drive
away the time till Falstaff come, I prithee, do thou
stand in some by-room while I question my puny drawer to
what end he gave me the sugar; and do thou never leave
calling “Francis,” that his tale to me may be nothing
but “Anon.” Step aside, and I’ll show thee a precedent.
Exit POINS

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: [within] Francis!

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Thou art perfect.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: [within] Francis!
Enter FRANCIS, a drawer

units.speaker: FRANCIS
units.text: Anon, anon, sir.—Look down into the Pomgarnet, Ralph.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Come hither, Francis.

units.speaker: FRANCIS
units.text: My lord?

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: How long hast thou to serve, Francis?

units.speaker: FRANCIS
units.text: Forsooth, five years, and as much as to—

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: [within] Francis!

units.speaker: FRANCIS
units.text: Anon, anon, sir.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Five year! By 'r lady, a long lease for the clinking of
pewter! But, Francis, darest thou be so valiant as to
play the coward with thy indenture, and show it a fair
pair of heels, and run from it?

units.speaker: FRANCIS
units.text:
O Lord, sir, I’ll be sworn upon all the books in
England, I could find in my heart—

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: [within] Francis!

units.speaker: FRANCIS
units.text: Anon, sir.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: How old art thou, Francis?

units.speaker: FRANCIS
units.text: Let me see. About Michaelmas next, I shall be—

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: [within] Francis!

units.speaker: FRANCIS
units.text:
Anon, sir. [To PRINCE HENRY ] Pray, stay a little, my
lord.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Nay, but hark you, Francis, for the sugar thou gavest
me,’twas a pennyworth, was ’t not?

units.speaker: FRANCIS
units.text: O Lord, I would it had been two!

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
I will give thee for it a thousand pound. Ask me when
thou wilt, and thou shalt have it.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: [within] Francis!

units.speaker: FRANCIS
units.text: Anon, anon.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Anon, Francis? No, Francis, but tomorrow, Francis; or,
Francis, o' Thursday; or indeed, Francis, when thou
wilt.
But, Francis—

units.speaker: FRANCIS
units.text: My lord?

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Wilt thou rob this leathern jerkin, crystal-button,
not-pated, agate-ring, puke-stocking, caddis-garter,
smooth-tongue, Spanish-pouch—

units.speaker: FRANCIS
units.text: O Lord, sir, who do you mean?

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Why, then, your brown bastard is your only drink, for
look you, Francis, your white canvas doublet will sully.
In Barbary, sir, it cannot come to so much.

units.speaker: FRANCIS
units.text: What, sir?

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: [within] Francis!

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Away, you rogue! Dost thou not hear them call?
Here they both call him. FRANCIS the drawer stands amazed, not knowing which way to go.
Enter VINTNER

units.speaker: VINTNER
units.text:
What, stand’st thou still and hear’st such a calling?
Look to the guests within.
Exit FRANCIS

units.speaker: VINTNER
units.text:
My lord, old Sir John with half a dozen more are at the
door.
Shall I let them in?

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Let them alone awhile, and then open the door.
Exit VINTNER

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Poins!
Reenter POINS

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: Anon, anon, sir.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Sirrah, Falstaff and the rest of the thieves are at the
door.
Shall we be merry?

units.speaker: POINS
units.text:
As merry as crickets, my lad. But hark you, what
cunning match have you made with this jest of the
drawer. Come, what’s the issue?

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
I am now of all humors that have showed themselves
humors since the old days of Goodman Adam to the pupil
age of this present twelve o'clock at midnight.
Enter FRANCIS

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: What’s o'clock, Francis?

units.speaker: FRANCIS
units.text: Anon, anon, sir.
Exit FRANCIS

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
That ever this fellow should have fewer words than a
parrot, and yet the son of a woman! His industry is
upstairs and downstairs, his eloquence the parcel of a
reckoning. I am not yet of Percy’s mind, the Hotspur of
the north, he that kills me some six or seven dozen of
Scots at a breakfast, washes his hands, and says to his
wife “Fie upon this quiet life! I want work.” “O my
sweet Harry,” says she, “how many hast thou killed
today?” “Give my roan horse a drench,” says he, and
answers “Some fourteen,” an hour after. “A trifle, a
trifle.” I prithee, call in Falstaff. I’ll play Percy,
and that damned brawn shall play Dame Mortimer his wife.
“Rivo!” says the drunkard. Call in Ribs, call in
Tallow.
Enter FALSTAFF, GADSHILL, PETO, and BARDOLPH, followed by FRANCIS with wine

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: Welcome, Jack. Where hast thou been?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
A plague of all cowards, I say, and a vengeance too!
Marry and amen!— Give me a cup of sack, boy.— Ere I lead
this life long, I’ll sew netherstocks and mend them, and
foot them too. A plague of all cowards!— Give me a cup
of sack, rogue! — Is there no virtue extant? (he
drinketh)

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Didst thou never see Titan kiss a dish of
butter?—Pitiful-hearted Titan!—that melted at the sweet
tale of the sun’s? If thou didst, then behold that
compound.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
(to FRANCIS ) You rogue, here’s lime in this sack too.—
There is nothing but roguery to be found in villanous
man, yet a coward is worse than a cup of sack with lime
in it. A villanous coward! Go thy ways, old Jack. Die
when thou wilt. If manhood, good manhood, be not forgot
upon the face of the earth, then am I a shotten herring.
There lives not three good men unhanged in England, and
one of them is fat and grows old, God help the while. A
bad world, I say. I would I were a weaver. I could sing
psalms, or anything. A plague of all cowards, I say
still.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: How now, woolsack, what mutter you?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
A King’s son! If I do not beat thee out of thy kingdom
with a dagger of lath, and drive all thy subjects afore
thee like a flock of wild geese, I’ll never wear hair on
my face more. You, Prince of Wales!

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Why, you whoreson round man, what’s the matter?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Are not you a coward? Answer me to that—and Poins
there?

units.speaker: POINS
units.text:
Zounds, ye fat paunch, an you call me coward, by the
Lord,
I’ll stab thee.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
I call thee coward? I’ll see thee damned ere I call
thee coward, but I would give a thousand pound I could
run as fast as thou canst. You are straight enough in
the shoulders you care not who sees your back. Call you
that backing of your friends? A plague upon such
backing! Give me them that will face me.— Give me a cup
of sack.—I am a rogue if I drunk today.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
O villain, thy lips are scarce wiped since thou
drunk’st last.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
All is one for that. [he drinketh] A plague of all
cowards, still say I.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: What’s the matter?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
What’s the matter? There be four of us here have ta'en
a thousand pound this day morning.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Where is it, Jack? Where is it?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Where is it? Taken from us it is. A hundred upon poor
four of us.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: What, a hundred, man?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
I am a rogue if I were not at half-sword with a dozen
of them two hours together. I have ’scaped by miracle. I
am eight times thrust through the doublet, four through
the hose, my buckler cut through and through, my sword
hacked like a handsaw. Ecce signum! I never dealt better
since I was a man. All would not do. A plague of all
cowards! (points to GADSHILL, PETO and BARDOLPH ) Let
them speak. If they speak more or less than truth, they
are villains, and the sons of darkness.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Speak, sirs, how was it?

units.speaker: GADSHILL
units.text: We four set upon some dozen.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Sixteen at least, my lord.

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text: And bound them.

units.speaker: PETO
units.text: No, no, they were not bound.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
You rogue, they were bound, every man of them, or I am
a
Jew else, an Ebrew Jew.

units.speaker: GADSHILL
units.text:
As we were sharing, some six or seven fresh men set
upon us.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: And unbound the rest, and then come in the other.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: What, fought you with them all?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
All? I know not what you call all, but if I fought not
with fifty of them I am a bunch of radish. If there were
not two- or three-and-fifty upon poor old Jack, then am
I no two-legged creature.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Pray God you have not murdered some of them.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Nay, that’s past praying for. I have peppered two of
them. Two I am sure I have paid, two rogues in buckram
suits. I tell thee what, Hal, if I tell thee a lie, spit
in my face, call me horse. Thou knowest my old ward.
Here I lay, and thus I bore my point. Four rogues in
buckram let drive at me.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: What, four? Thou saidst but two even now.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Four, Hal, I told thee four.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: Ay, ay, he said four.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
These four came all afront, and mainly thrust at me. I
made me no more ado, but took all their seven points in
my target, thus.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Seven? Why there were but four even now.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: In buckram?

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: Ay, four in buckram suits.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Seven, by these hilts, or I am a villain else.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
[aside to POINS ] Prithee, let him alone. We shall have
more anon.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Dost thou hear me, Hal?

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Ay, and mark thee too, Jack.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Do so, for it is worth the listening to. These nine in
buckram that I told thee of—

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: So, two more already.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Their points being broken—

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: Down fell their hose.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Began to give me ground, but I followed me close, came
in foot and hand, and with a thought, seven of the
eleven I paid.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: O monstrous! Eleven buckram men grown out of two!

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
But as the devil would have it, three misbegotten
knaves in Kendal green came at my back, and let drive at
me, for it was so dark, Hal, that thou couldst not see
thy hand.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
These lies are like their father that begets them,
gross as a mountain, open, palpable. Why, thou
claybrained guts, thou knotty-pated fool, thou whoreson,
obscene, greasy tallow-catch—

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
What, art thou mad? Art thou mad? Is not the truth the
truth?

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Why, how couldst thou know these men in Kendal green,
when it was so dark thou couldst not see thy hand? Come,
tell us your reason. What sayest thou to this?

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: Come, your reason, Jack, your reason.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
What, upon compulsion? Zounds, an I were at the
strappado or all the racks in the world, I would not
tell you on compulsion. Give you a reason on compulsion?
If reasons were as plentiful as blackberries, I would
give no man a reason upon compulsion, I.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
I’ll be no longer guilty of this sin. This sanguine
coward, this bed-presser, this horseback-breaker, this
huge hill of flesh—

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
'Sblood, you starveling, you elfskin, you dried neat’s
tongue, you bull’s pizzle, you stockfish! O, for breath
to utter what is like thee! You tailor’s-yard, you
sheath, you bowcase, you vile standing tuck—

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Well, breathe awhile, and then to it again, and when
thou hast tired thyself in base comparisons, hear me
speak but this.

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: Mark, Jack.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
We two saw you four set on four, and bound them and
were masters of their wealth. Mark now how a plain tale
shall put you down. Then did we two set on you four and,
with a word, outfaced you from your prize, and have it;
yea, and can show it you here in the house. And,
Falstaff, you carried your guts away as nimbly, with as
quick dexterity, and roared for mercy, and still run and
roared, as ever I heard bull-calf. What a slave art
thou to hack thy sword as thou hast done, and then say
it was in fight! What trick, what device, what
starting-hole canst thou now find out to hide thee from
this open and apparent shame?

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: Come, let’s hear, Jack. What trick hast thou now?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
By the Lord, I knew you as well as he that made you.
Why, hear you, my masters, was it for me to kill the heir
apparent? Should I turn upon the true Prince? Why, thou
knowest I am as valiant as Hercules, but beware
instinct. The lion will not touch the true Prince.
Instinct is a great matter. I was now a coward on
instinct. I shall think the better of myself, and thee,
during my life—I for a valiant lion, and thou for a true
Prince. But, by the Lord, lads, I am glad you have the
money.—Hostess, clap to the doors.— Watch tonight, pray
to-morrow. Gallants, lads, boys, hearts of gold, all the
titles of good fellowship come to you. What, shall we
be merry? hall we have a play extempore?

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Content, and the argument shall be thy running away.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Ah, no more of that, Hal, an thou lovest me.
Enter MISTRESS QUICKLY

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: O Jesu, my lord the Prince!

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: How now, my lady the hostess, what sayest thou to me?

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
Marry, my lord, there is a nobleman of the court at
door would speak with you. He says he comes from your
father.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Give him as much as will make him a royal man and send
him back again to my mother.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: What manner of man is he?

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: An old man.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
What doth Gravity out of his bed at midnight? Shall I
give him his answer?

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Prithee do, Jack.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Faith, and I’ll send him packing.
Exit FALSTAFF

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Now, sirs. By 'r lady, you fought fair.—So did you,
Peto.— So did you, Bardolph.—You are lions too. You ran
away upon instinct. You will not touch the true Prince.
No, fie!

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text: Faith, I ran when I saw others run.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Faith, tell me now in earnest, how came Falstaff’s
sword so hacked?

units.speaker: PETO
units.text:
Why, he hacked it with his dagger and said he would
swear truth out of England but he would make you believe
it was done in fight, and persuaded us to do the like.

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text:
Yea, and to tickle our noses with speargrass to make
them bleed, and then to beslubber our garments with it,
and swear it was the blood of true men. I did that I did
not this seven year before: I blushed to hear his
monstrous devices.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
O villain, thou stolest a cup of sack eighteen years
ago, and wert taken with the manner, and ever since thou
hast blushed extempore. Thou hadst fire and sword on
thy side, and yet thou ran’st away. What instinct hadst
thou for it?

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text:
My lord, do you see these meteors? Do you behold these
exhalations?

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: I do.

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text: What think you they portend?

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Hot livers and cold purses.

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text: Choler, my lord, if rightly taken.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: No, if rightly taken, halter.
Enter FALSTAFF

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Here comes lean Jack. Here comes bare-bone.—How now, my
sweet creature of bombast? How long is ’t ago, Jack,
since thou sawest thine own knee?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
My own knee? When I was about thy years, Hal, I was not
an eagle’s talon in the waist. I could have crept into
any alderman’s thumb-ring. A plague of sighing and
grief! It blows a man up like a bladder. There’s
villanous news abroad. Here was Sir John Bracy from your
father. You must to the court in the morning. That same
mad fellow of the north, Percy, and he of Wales that
gave Amamon the bastinado, and made Lucifer cuckold, and
swore the devil his true liegeman upon the cross of a
Welsh hook—what a plague call you him?

units.speaker: POINS
units.text: Owen Glendower.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Owen, Owen, the same, and his son-in-law Mortimer, and
old Northumberland, and that sprightly Scot of Scots,
Douglas, that runs a-horseback up a hill perpendicular—

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
He that rides at high speed, and with his pistol kills
a sparrow flying.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: You have hit it.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: So did he never the sparrow.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Well, that rascal hath good mettle in him. He will not
run.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Why, what a rascal art thou then to praise him so for
running?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
A-horseback, you cuckoo, but afoot he will not budge a
foot.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Yes, Jack, upon instinct.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
I grant you, upon instinct. Well, he is there too, and
one Mordake, and a thousand blue-caps more: Worcester is
stolen away tonight. Thy father’s beard is turned white
with the news. You may buy land now as cheap as
stinking mackerel.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Why, then, it is like if there come a hot June, and
this civil buffeting hold, we shall buy maidenheads as
they buy hob-nails, by the hundreds.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
By the Mass, thou sayest true. It is like we shall have
good trading that way. But tell me, Hal, art not thou
horrible afeard? Thou being heir apparent, could the
world pick thee out three such enemies again as that
fiend Douglas, that spirit Percy, and that devil
Glendower? Art thou not horribly afraid? Doth not thy
blood thrill at it?

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Not a whit, i' faith; I lack some of thy instinct.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Well, thou wert be horribly chid tomorrow when thou
comest to thy father. If thou love me, practice an
answer.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Do thou stand for my father and examine me upon the
particulars of my life.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Shall I? Content. This chair shall be my state, this
dagger my scepter, and this cushion my crown.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Thy state is taken for a joined stool, thy golden
scepter for a leaden dagger, and thy precious rich crown
for a pitiful bald crown.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Well, an the fire of grace be not quite out of thee,
now shalt thou be moved.—Give me a cup of sack to make
my eyes look red, that it may be thought I have wept,
for I must speak in passion, and I will do it in King
Cambyses' vein.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Well, here is my leg.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: And here is my speech. Stand aside, nobility.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: O Jesu, this is excellent sport, i' faith!

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: Weep not, sweet queen, for trickling tears are vain.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: O the father, how he holds his countenance!

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
For God’s sake, lords, convey my tristful queen,
For tears do stop the floodgates of her eyes.

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
O Jesu, he doth it as like one of these harlotry
players as ever
I see.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Peace, good pint-pot. Peace, good tickle-brain.— (to
PRINCE HENRY ) Harry, I do not only marvel where thou
spendest thy time, but also how thou art accompanied.
For though the camomile, the more it is trodden on, the
faster it grows, so youth, the more it is wasted, the
sooner it wears. That thou art my son I have partly thy
mother’s word, partly my own opinion, but chiefly a
villanous trick of thine eye and a foolish-hanging of
thy nether lip that doth warrant me. If then thou be son
to me, here lies the point: why, being son to me, art
thou so pointed at? Shall the blessed sun of heaven
prove a micher and eat blackberries? A question not to
be asked. Shall the sun of England prove a thief and
take purses? A question to be asked. There is a thing,
Harry, which thou hast often heard of, and it is known
to many in our land by the name of pitch: this pitch, as
ancient writers do report, doth defile; so doth the
company thou keepest. For, Harry, now I do not speak to
thee, in drink but in tears; not in pleasure, but in
passion; not in words only, but in woes also. And yet
there is a virtuous man whom I have often noted in thy
company, but I know not his name.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: What manner of man, an it like your Majesty?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
A goodly portly man, i' faith, and a corpulent; of a
cheerful look, a pleasing eye, and a most noble
carriage, and, as I think, his age some fifty, or, by 'r
Lady, inclining to three score; and now I remember me,
his name is Falstaff. If that man should be lewdly
given, he deceiveth me, for, Harry, I see virtue in his
looks. If then the tree may be known by the fruit, as
the fruit by the tree, then peremptorily I speak it:
there is virtue in that Falstaff; him keep with, the
rest banish. And tell me now, thou naughty varlet, tell
me, where hast thou been this month?

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Dost thou speak like a king? Do thou stand for me, and
I’ll play my father.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Depose me? If thou dost it half so gravely, so
majestically, both in word and matter, hang me up by the
heels for a rabbit-sucker or a poulter’s hare.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Well, here I am set.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: And here I stand. — [To the others] Judge, my masters.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Now, Harry, whence come you?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: My noble lord, from Eastcheap.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: The complaints I hear of thee are grievous.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
'Sblood, my lord, they are false.— [To the others] Nay,
I’ll tickle you for a young prince, i' faith.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Swearest thou? Ungracious boy, henceforth ne'er look on
me. Thou art violently carried away from grace. There
is a devil haunts thee in the likeness of an old fat
man. A tun of man is thy companion. Why dost thou
converse with that trunk of humors, that bolting-hutch
of beastliness, that swollen parcel of dropsies, that
huge bombard of sack, that stuffed cloakbag of guts,
that roasted Manningtree ox with the pudding in his
belly, that reverend Vice, that gray iniquity, that
father ruffian, that vanity in years? Wherein is he
good, but to taste sack and drink it? Wherein neat and
cleanly but to carve a capon and eat it? Wherein cunning
but in craft? Wherein crafty but in villany? Wherein
villanous but in all things? Wherein worthy but in
nothing?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
I would your Grace would take me with you. Whom means
your Grace?

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
That villanous abominable misleader of youth, Falstaff,
that old white-bearded Satan.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text: My lord, the man I know.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: I know thou dost.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
But to say I know more harm in him than in myself were
to say more than I know. That he is old, the more the
pity; his white hairs do witness it. But that he is,
saving your reverence, a whoremaster, that I utterly
deny. If sack and sugar be a fault, God help the wicked.
If to be old and merry be a sin, then many an old host
that I know is damned. If to be fat be to be hated, then
Pharaoh’s lean kine are to be loved. No, my good lord,
banish Peto, banish Bardolph, banish Poins, but for
sweet Jack Falstaff, kind Jack Falstaff, true Jack
Falstaff, valiant Jack Falstaff, and therefore more
valiant being, as he is old Jack Falstaff, banish not
him thy Harry’s company, banish not him thy Harry’s
company. Banish plump Jack, and banish all the world.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: I do, I will.
Knocking within. Exeunt BARDOLPH, MISTRESS QUICKLY, and FRANCIS. Enter BARDOLPH, running

units.speaker: BARDOLPH
units.text:
O, my lord, my lord, the Sheriff with a most monstrous
watch is at the door.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Out, you rogue.—Play out the play. I have much to say
in the behalf of that Falstaff.
Enter MISTRESS QUICKLY

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text: O Jesu, my lord, my lord—

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Heigh, heigh, the devil rides upon a fiddlestick.
What’s the matter?

units.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
units.text:
The Sheriff and all the watch are at the door. They are
come to search the house. Shall I let them in?

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Dost thou hear, Hal? Never call a true piece of gold a
counterfeit. Thou art essentially made, without seeming
so.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: And thou a natural coward without instinct.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
I deny your major. If you will deny the Sheriff, so; if
not, let him enter. If I become not a cart as well as
another man, a plague on my bringing up. I hope I shall
as soon be strangled with a halter as another.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
Go, hide thee behind the arras. The rest walk up
above.—
Now, my masters, for a true face and good conscience.

units.speaker: FALSTAFF
units.text:
Both which I have had, but their date is out; and
therefore I’ll hide me. [he hides behind the arras]
Exeunt all but PRINCE HENRY and PETO

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Call in the Sheriff.
Enter SHERIFF and the CARRIER

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Now, Master Sheriff, what is your will with me?

units.speaker: SHERIFF
units.text:
First pardon me, my lord. A hue and cry
Hath followed certain men unto this house.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: What men?

units.speaker: SHERIFF
units.text:
One of them is well known, my gracious lord,
A gross fat man.

units.speaker: CARRIER
units.text: As fat as butter.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
The man, I do assure you is not here,
For I myself at this time have employed him.
And, Sheriff, I will engage my word to thee
That I will by tomorrow dinner time
Send him to answer thee or any man
For any thing he shall be charged withal.
And so let me entreat you leave the house.

units.speaker: SHERIFF
units.text:
I will, my lord. There are two gentlemen
Have in this robbery lost three hundred marks.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
It may be so. If he have robbed these men,
He shall be answerable; and so farewell.

units.speaker: SHERIFF
units.text: Good night, my noble lord.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: I think it is good morrow, is it not?

units.speaker: SHERIFF
units.text: Indeed, my lord, I think it be two o'clock.
Exeunt SHERIFF and CARRIER

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text:
This oily rascal is known as well as Paul’s. Go call
him forth.

units.speaker: PETO
units.text:
Falstaff!— [pulls back the arras] Fast asleep behind
the arras, and snorting like a horse.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Hark, how hard he fetches breath. Search his pockets.

units.speaker: PETO
units.text: Nothing but papers, my lord.

units.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
units.text: Let’s see what they be. Read them.

units.speaker: PETO
units.text:
(reads) Item, a capon, … 2s. 2d.
Item, sauce, … 4d.
Item, sack, two gallons, … 5s. 8d.
