sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Didst thou never see Titan kiss a dish of
butter?—Pitiful-hearted Titan!—that melted at the sweet
tale of the sun’s? If thou didst, then behold that
compound.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Didst thou never see Titan kiss a dish of
butter?—Pitiful-hearted Titan!—that melted at the sweet
tale of the sun’s? If thou didst, then behold that
compound.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
(to FRANCIS ) You rogue, here’s lime in this sack too.—
There is nothing but roguery to be found in villanous
man, yet a coward is worse than a cup of sack with lime
in it. A villanous coward! Go thy ways, old Jack. Die
when thou wilt. If manhood, good manhood, be not forgot
upon the face of the earth, then am I a shotten herring.
There lives not three good men unhanged in England, and
one of them is fat and grows old, God help the while. A
bad world, I say. I would I were a weaver. I could sing
psalms, or anything. A plague of all cowards, I say
still.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: How now, woolsack, what mutter you?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
A King’s son! If I do not beat thee out of thy kingdom
with a dagger of lath, and drive all thy subjects afore
thee like a flock of wild geese, I’ll never wear hair on
my face more. You, Prince of Wales!

my face more. You, Prince of Wales!

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Why, you whoreson round man, what’s the matter?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Are not you a coward? Answer me to that—and Poins
there?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Are not you a coward? Answer me to that—and Poins
there?

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text:
Zounds, ye fat paunch, an you call me coward, by the
Lord,
I’ll stab thee.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
I call thee coward? I’ll see thee damned ere I call
thee coward, but I would give a thousand pound I could
run as fast as thou canst. You are straight enough in
the shoulders you care not who sees your back. Call you
that backing of your friends? A plague upon such
backing! Give me them that will face me.— Give me a cup
of sack.—I am a rogue if I drunk today.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
O villain, thy lips are scarce wiped since thou
drunk’st last.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
O villain, thy lips are scarce wiped since thou
drunk’st last.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
All is one for that. [he drinketh] A plague of all
cowards, still say I.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: What’s the matter?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
What’s the matter? There be four of us here have ta'en
a thousand pound this day morning.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
What’s the matter? There be four of us here have ta'en
a thousand pound this day morning.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Where is it, Jack? Where is it?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Where is it, Jack? Where is it?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Where is it? Taken from us it is. A hundred upon poor
four of us.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Where is it? Taken from us it is. A hundred upon poor
four of us.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Where is it? Taken from us it is. A hundred upon poor
four of us.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: What, a hundred, man?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: What, a hundred, man?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
I am a rogue if I were not at half-sword with a dozen
of them two hours together. I have ’scaped by miracle. I
am eight times thrust through the doublet, four through
the hose, my buckler cut through and through, my sword
hacked like a handsaw. Ecce signum! I never dealt better
since I was a man. All would not do. A plague of all
cowards! (points to GADSHILL, PETO and BARDOLPH ) Let
them speak. If they speak more or less than truth, they
are villains, and the sons of darkness.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Speak, sirs, how was it?

sentences.speaker: GADSHILL
sentences.text: We four set upon some dozen.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Sixteen at least, my lord.

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text: And bound them.

sentences.speaker: PETO
sentences.text: No, no, they were not bound.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
You rogue, they were bound, every man of them, or I am
a
Jew else, an Ebrew Jew.

sentences.speaker: GADSHILL
sentences.text:
As we were sharing, some six or seven fresh men set
upon us.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: And unbound the rest, and then come in the other.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: What, fought you with them all?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: What, fought you with them all?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
All? I know not what you call all, but if I fought not
with fifty of them I am a bunch of radish. If there were
not two- or three-and-fifty upon poor old Jack, then am
I no two-legged creature.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
All? I know not what you call all, but if I fought not
with fifty of them I am a bunch of radish. If there were
not two- or three-and-fifty upon poor old Jack, then am
I no two-legged creature.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
All? I know not what you call all, but if I fought not
with fifty of them I am a bunch of radish. If there were
not two- or three-and-fifty upon poor old Jack, then am
I no two-legged creature.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Pray God you have not murdered some of them.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Nay, that’s past praying for. I have peppered two of
them. Two I am sure I have paid, two rogues in buckram
suits. I tell thee what, Hal, if I tell thee a lie, spit
in my face, call me horse. Thou knowest my old ward.
Here I lay, and thus I bore my point. Four rogues in
buckram let drive at me.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: What, four? Thou saidst but two even now.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: What, four? Thou saidst but two even now.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Four, Hal, I told thee four.

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text: Ay, ay, he said four.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
These four came all afront, and mainly thrust at me. I
made me no more ado, but took all their seven points in
my target, thus.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
These four came all afront, and mainly thrust at me. I
made me no more ado, but took all their seven points in
my target, thus.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Seven? Why there were but four even now.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Seven? Why there were but four even now.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: In buckram?

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text: Ay, four in buckram suits.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Seven, by these hilts, or I am a villain else.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Seven, by these hilts, or I am a villain else.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
[aside to POINS ] Prithee, let him alone. We shall have
more anon.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
[aside to POINS ] Prithee, let him alone. We shall have
more anon.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Dost thou hear me, Hal?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Ay, and mark thee too, Jack.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Do so, for it is worth the listening to. These nine in
buckram that I told thee of—

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Do so, for it is worth the listening to. These nine in
buckram that I told thee of—

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: So, two more already.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Their points being broken—

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text: Down fell their hose.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Began to give me ground, but I followed me close, came
in foot and hand, and with a thought, seven of the
eleven I paid.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: O monstrous! Eleven buckram men grown out of two!

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: O monstrous! Eleven buckram men grown out of two!

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
But as the devil would have it, three misbegotten
knaves in Kendal green came at my back, and let drive at
me, for it was so dark, Hal, that thou couldst not see
thy hand.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
But as the devil would have it, three misbegotten
knaves in Kendal green came at my back, and let drive at
me, for it was so dark, Hal, that thou couldst not see
thy hand.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
These lies are like their father that begets them,
gross as a mountain, open, palpable. Why, thou
claybrained guts, thou knotty-pated fool, thou whoreson,
obscene, greasy tallow-catch—

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
These lies are like their father that begets them,
gross as a mountain, open, palpable. Why, thou
claybrained guts, thou knotty-pated fool, thou whoreson,
obscene, greasy tallow-catch—

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
What, art thou mad? Art thou mad? Is not the truth the
truth?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
What, art thou mad? Art thou mad? Is not the truth the
truth?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
What, art thou mad? Art thou mad? Is not the truth the
truth?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Why, how couldst thou know these men in Kendal green,
when it was so dark thou couldst not see thy hand? Come,
tell us your reason. What sayest thou to this?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:

when it was so dark thou couldst not see thy hand? Come,
tell us your reason. What sayest thou to this?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text: Come, your reason, Jack, your reason.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
What, upon compulsion? Zounds, an I were at the
strappado or all the racks in the world, I would not
tell you on compulsion. Give you a reason on compulsion?
If reasons were as plentiful as blackberries, I would
give no man a reason upon compulsion, I.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
I’ll be no longer guilty of this sin. This sanguine
coward, this bed-presser, this horseback-breaker, this
huge hill of flesh—

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
I’ll be no longer guilty of this sin. This sanguine
coward, this bed-presser, this horseback-breaker, this
huge hill of flesh—

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
'Sblood, you starveling, you elfskin, you dried neat’s
tongue, you bull’s pizzle, you stockfish! O, for breath
to utter what is like thee! You tailor’s-yard, you
sheath, you bowcase, you vile standing tuck—

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Well, breathe awhile, and then to it again, and when
thou hast tired thyself in base comparisons, hear me
speak but this.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Well, breathe awhile, and then to it again, and when
thou hast tired thyself in base comparisons, hear me
speak but this.

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text: Mark, Jack.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
We two saw you four set on four, and bound them and
were masters of their wealth. Mark now how a plain tale
shall put you down. Then did we two set on you four and,
with a word, outfaced you from your prize, and have it;
yea, and can show it you here in the house. And,
Falstaff, you carried your guts away as nimbly, with as
quick dexterity, and roared for mercy, and still run and
roared, as ever I heard bull-calf. What a slave art
thou to hack thy sword as thou hast done, and then say
it was in fight! What trick, what device, what
starting-hole canst thou now find out to hide thee from
this open and apparent shame?

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text: Come, let’s hear, Jack. What trick hast thou now?

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text: Come, let’s hear, Jack. What trick hast thou now?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
By the Lord, I knew you as well as he that made you.
Why, hear you, my masters, was it for me to kill the heir
apparent? Should I turn upon the true Prince? Why, thou
knowest I am as valiant as Hercules, but beware
instinct. The lion will not touch the true Prince.
Instinct is a great matter. I was now a coward on
instinct. I shall think the better of myself, and thee,
during my life—I for a valiant lion, and thou for a true
Prince. But, by the Lord, lads, I am glad you have the
money.—Hostess, clap to the doors.— Watch tonight, pray
to-morrow. Gallants, lads, boys, hearts of gold, all the
titles of good fellowship come to you. What, shall we
be merry? hall we have a play extempore?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Content, and the argument shall be thy running away.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Ah, no more of that, Hal, an thou lovest me.
Enter MISTRESS QUICKLY

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text: O Jesu, my lord the Prince!

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: How now, my lady the hostess, what sayest thou to me?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: How now, my lady the hostess, what sayest thou to me?

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
Marry, my lord, there is a nobleman of the court at
door would speak with you. He says he comes from your
father.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
Marry, my lord, there is a nobleman of the court at
door would speak with you. He says he comes from your
father.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Give him as much as will make him a royal man and send
him back again to my mother.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: What manner of man is he?

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text: An old man.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
What doth Gravity out of his bed at midnight? Shall I
give him his answer?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
What doth Gravity out of his bed at midnight? Shall I
give him his answer?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Prithee do, Jack.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Faith, and I’ll send him packing.
Exit FALSTAFF

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Now, sirs. By 'r lady, you fought fair.—So did you,
Peto.— So did you, Bardolph.—You are lions too. You ran
away upon instinct. You will not touch the true Prince.
No, fie!

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text: Faith, I ran when I saw others run.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Faith, tell me now in earnest, how came Falstaff’s
sword so hacked?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Faith, tell me now in earnest, how came Falstaff’s
sword so hacked?

sentences.speaker: PETO
sentences.text:
Why, he hacked it with his dagger and said he would
swear truth out of England but he would make you believe
it was done in fight, and persuaded us to do the like.

sentences.speaker: PETO
sentences.text:
Why, he hacked it with his dagger and said he would
swear truth out of England but he would make you believe
it was done in fight, and persuaded us to do the like.

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text:
Yea, and to tickle our noses with speargrass to make
them bleed, and then to beslubber our garments with it,
and swear it was the blood of true men. I did that I did
not this seven year before: I blushed to hear his
monstrous devices.

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
O villain, thou stolest a cup of sack eighteen years
ago, and wert taken with the manner, and ever since thou
hast blushed extempore. Thou hadst fire and sword on
thy side, and yet thou ran’st away. What instinct hadst
thou for it?

hast blushed extempore. Thou hadst fire and sword on
thy side, and yet thou ran’st away. What instinct hadst
thou for it?

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text:
My lord, do you see these meteors? Do you behold these
exhalations?

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text:
My lord, do you see these meteors? Do you behold these
exhalations?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: I do.

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text: What think you they portend?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Hot livers and cold purses.

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text: Choler, my lord, if rightly taken.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: No, if rightly taken, halter.
Enter FALSTAFF

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Here comes lean Jack. Here comes bare-bone.—How now, my
sweet creature of bombast? How long is ’t ago, Jack,
since thou sawest thine own knee?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Here comes lean Jack. Here comes bare-bone.—How now, my
sweet creature of bombast? How long is ’t ago, Jack,
since thou sawest thine own knee?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Here comes lean Jack. Here comes bare-bone.—How now, my
sweet creature of bombast? How long is ’t ago, Jack,
since thou sawest thine own knee?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Here comes lean Jack. Here comes bare-bone.—How now, my
sweet creature of bombast? How long is ’t ago, Jack,
since thou sawest thine own knee?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
My own knee? When I was about thy years, Hal, I was not
an eagle’s talon in the waist. I could have crept into
any alderman’s thumb-ring. A plague of sighing and
grief! It blows a man up like a bladder. There’s
villanous news abroad. Here was Sir John Bracy from your
father. You must to the court in the morning. That same
mad fellow of the north, Percy, and he of Wales that
gave Amamon the bastinado, and made Lucifer cuckold, and
swore the devil his true liegeman upon the cross of a
Welsh hook—what a plague call you him?

sentences.speaker: POINS
sentences.text: Owen Glendower.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Owen, Owen, the same, and his son-in-law Mortimer, and
old Northumberland, and that sprightly Scot of Scots,
Douglas, that runs a-horseback up a hill perpendicular—

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Owen, Owen, the same, and his son-in-law Mortimer, and
old Northumberland, and that sprightly Scot of Scots,
Douglas, that runs a-horseback up a hill perpendicular—

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
He that rides at high speed, and with his pistol kills
a sparrow flying.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: You have hit it.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: So did he never the sparrow.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Well, that rascal hath good mettle in him. He will not
run.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Well, that rascal hath good mettle in him. He will not
run.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Why, what a rascal art thou then to praise him so for
running?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Why, what a rascal art thou then to praise him so for
running?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
A-horseback, you cuckoo, but afoot he will not budge a
foot.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Yes, Jack, upon instinct.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
I grant you, upon instinct. Well, he is there too, and
one Mordake, and a thousand blue-caps more: Worcester is
stolen away tonight. Thy father’s beard is turned white
with the news. You may buy land now as cheap as
stinking mackerel.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:

stolen away tonight. Thy father’s beard is turned white

stinking mackerel.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Why, then, it is like if there come a hot June, and
this civil buffeting hold, we shall buy maidenheads as
they buy hob-nails, by the hundreds.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
By the Mass, thou sayest true. It is like we shall have
good trading that way. But tell me, Hal, art not thou
horrible afeard? Thou being heir apparent, could the
world pick thee out three such enemies again as that
fiend Douglas, that spirit Percy, and that devil
Glendower? Art thou not horribly afraid? Doth not thy
blood thrill at it?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
By the Mass, thou sayest true. It is like we shall have

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Not a whit, i' faith; I lack some of thy instinct.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Not a whit, i' faith; I lack some of thy instinct.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Well, thou wert be horribly chid tomorrow when thou
comest to thy father. If thou love me, practice an
answer.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Well, thou wert be horribly chid tomorrow when thou
comest to thy father. If thou love me, practice an
answer.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Do thou stand for my father and examine me upon the
particulars of my life.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Shall I? Content. This chair shall be my state, this
dagger my scepter, and this cushion my crown.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Shall I? Content. This chair shall be my state, this
dagger my scepter, and this cushion my crown.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Shall I? Content. This chair shall be my state, this
dagger my scepter, and this cushion my crown.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Thy state is taken for a joined stool, thy golden
scepter for a leaden dagger, and thy precious rich crown
for a pitiful bald crown.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Well, an the fire of grace be not quite out of thee,
now shalt thou be moved.—Give me a cup of sack to make
my eyes look red, that it may be thought I have wept,
for I must speak in passion, and I will do it in King
Cambyses' vein.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Well, an the fire of grace be not quite out of thee,
now shalt thou be moved.—Give me a cup of sack to make
my eyes look red, that it may be thought I have wept,
for I must speak in passion, and I will do it in King
Cambyses' vein.

sentences.text:

now shalt thou be moved.—Give me a cup of sack to make
my eyes look red, that it may be thought I have wept,
for I must speak in passion, and I will do it in King
Cambyses' vein.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Well, here is my leg.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: And here is my speech. Stand aside, nobility.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: And here is my speech. Stand aside, nobility.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text: O Jesu, this is excellent sport, i' faith!

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Weep not, sweet queen, for trickling tears are vain.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text: O the father, how he holds his countenance!

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
For God’s sake, lords, convey my tristful queen,
For tears do stop the floodgates of her eyes.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
For God’s sake, lords, convey my tristful queen,
For tears do stop the floodgates of her eyes.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
O Jesu, he doth it as like one of these harlotry
players as ever
I see.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Peace, good pint-pot. Peace, good tickle-brain.— (to
PRINCE HENRY ) Harry, I do not only marvel where thou
spendest thy time, but also how thou art accompanied.
For though the camomile, the more it is trodden on, the
faster it grows, so youth, the more it is wasted, the
sooner it wears. That thou art my son I have partly thy
mother’s word, partly my own opinion, but chiefly a
villanous trick of thine eye and a foolish-hanging of
thy nether lip that doth warrant me. If then thou be son
to me, here lies the point: why, being son to me, art
thou so pointed at? Shall the blessed sun of heaven
prove a micher and eat blackberries? A question not to
be asked. Shall the sun of England prove a thief and
take purses? A question to be asked. There is a thing,
Harry, which thou hast often heard of, and it is known
to many in our land by the name of pitch: this pitch, as
ancient writers do report, doth defile; so doth the
company thou keepest. For, Harry, now I do not speak to
thee, in drink but in tears; not in pleasure, but in
passion; not in words only, but in woes also. And yet
there is a virtuous man whom I have often noted in thy
company, but I know not his name.

company thou keepest. For, Harry, now I do not speak to

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Peace, good pint-pot. Peace, good tickle-brain.— (to
PRINCE HENRY ) Harry, I do not only marvel where thou
spendest thy time, but also how thou art accompanied.
For though the camomile, the more it is trodden on, the
faster it grows, so youth, the more it is wasted, the
sooner it wears. That thou art my son I have partly thy
mother’s word, partly my own opinion, but chiefly a
villanous trick of thine eye and a foolish-hanging of
thy nether lip that doth warrant me. If then thou be son
to me, here lies the point: why, being son to me, art
thou so pointed at? Shall the blessed sun of heaven
prove a micher and eat blackberries? A question not to
be asked. Shall the sun of England prove a thief and
take purses? A question to be asked. There is a thing,

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: What manner of man, an it like your Majesty?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
A goodly portly man, i' faith, and a corpulent; of a
cheerful look, a pleasing eye, and a most noble
carriage, and, as I think, his age some fifty, or, by 'r
Lady, inclining to three score; and now I remember me,
his name is Falstaff. If that man should be lewdly
given, he deceiveth me, for, Harry, I see virtue in his
looks. If then the tree may be known by the fruit, as
the fruit by the tree, then peremptorily I speak it:
there is virtue in that Falstaff; him keep with, the
rest banish. And tell me now, thou naughty varlet, tell
me, where hast thou been this month?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Dost thou speak like a king? Do thou stand for me, and
I’ll play my father.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Dost thou speak like a king? Do thou stand for me, and
I’ll play my father.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Depose me? If thou dost it half so gravely, so
majestically, both in word and matter, hang me up by the
heels for a rabbit-sucker or a poulter’s hare.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Depose me? If thou dost it half so gravely, so
majestically, both in word and matter, hang me up by the
heels for a rabbit-sucker or a poulter’s hare.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Well, here I am set.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: And here I stand. — [To the others] Judge, my masters.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Now, Harry, whence come you?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: My noble lord, from Eastcheap.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: The complaints I hear of thee are grievous.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
'Sblood, my lord, they are false.— [To the others] Nay,
I’ll tickle you for a young prince, i' faith.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Swearest thou? Ungracious boy, henceforth ne'er look on
me. Thou art violently carried away from grace. There
is a devil haunts thee in the likeness of an old fat
man. A tun of man is thy companion. Why dost thou
converse with that trunk of humors, that bolting-hutch
of beastliness, that swollen parcel of dropsies, that
huge bombard of sack, that stuffed cloakbag of guts,
that roasted Manningtree ox with the pudding in his
belly, that reverend Vice, that gray iniquity, that
father ruffian, that vanity in years? Wherein is he
good, but to taste sack and drink it? Wherein neat and
cleanly but to carve a capon and eat it? Wherein cunning
but in craft? Wherein crafty but in villany? Wherein
villanous but in all things? Wherein worthy but in
nothing?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
I would your Grace would take me with you. Whom means
your Grace?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
I would your Grace would take me with you. Whom means
your Grace?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
That villanous abominable misleader of youth, Falstaff,
that old white-bearded Satan.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: My lord, the man I know.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: I know thou dost.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
But to say I know more harm in him than in myself were
to say more than I know. That he is old, the more the
pity; his white hairs do witness it. But that he is,
saving your reverence, a whoremaster, that I utterly
deny. If sack and sugar be a fault, God help the wicked.
If to be old and merry be a sin, then many an old host
that I know is damned. If to be fat be to be hated, then
Pharaoh’s lean kine are to be loved. No, my good lord,
banish Peto, banish Bardolph, banish Poins, but for
sweet Jack Falstaff, kind Jack Falstaff, true Jack
Falstaff, valiant Jack Falstaff, and therefore more
valiant being, as he is old Jack Falstaff, banish not
him thy Harry’s company, banish not him thy Harry’s
company. Banish plump Jack, and banish all the world.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
But to say I know more harm in him than in myself were
to say more than I know. That he is old, the more the
pity; his white hairs do witness it. But that he is,
saving your reverence, a whoremaster, that I utterly
deny. If sack and sugar be a fault, God help the wicked.
If to be old and merry be a sin, then many an old host
that I know is damned. If to be fat be to be hated, then
Pharaoh’s lean kine are to be loved. No, my good lord,
banish Peto, banish Bardolph, banish Poins, but for
sweet Jack Falstaff, kind Jack Falstaff, true Jack
Falstaff, valiant Jack Falstaff, and therefore more
valiant being, as he is old Jack Falstaff, banish not
him thy Harry’s company, banish not him thy Harry’s
company. Banish plump Jack, and banish all the world.

Dost thou hear, Hal? Never call a true piece of gold a

not, let him enter. If I become not a cart as well as
another man, a plague on my bringing up. I hope I shall
as soon be strangled with a halter as another.

First pardon me, my lord. A hue and cry

sentences.speaker: SHERIFF

First pardon me, my lord. A hue and cry

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY

sentences.speaker: SHERIFF

One of them is well known, my gracious lord,
A gross fat man.

sentences.speaker: SHERIFF
sentences.text:
One of them is well known, my gracious lord,
A gross fat man.

sentences.speaker: CARRIER

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
The man, I do assure you is not here,
For I myself at this time have employed him.
And, Sheriff, I will engage my word to thee
That I will by tomorrow dinner time
Send him to answer thee or any man
For any thing he shall be charged withal.
And so let me entreat you leave the house.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
O monstrous! But one halfpennyworth of bread to this
intolerable deal of sack! What there is else, keep
close. We’ll read it at more advantage. There let him
sleep till day. I’ll to the court in the morning. We
must all to the wars, and thy place shall be honorable.
I’ll procure this fat rogue a charge of foot, and I know
his death will be a march of twelve score. The money
shall be paid back again with advantage. Be with me
betimes in the morning, and so good morrow, Peto.

Enter HOTSPUR,WORCESTER, Lord MORTIMER, and Owen GLENDOWER

sentences.speaker: MORTIMER
sentences.text:
These promises are fair, the parties sure,
And our induction full of prosperous hope.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
Lord Mortimer and cousin Glendower,
Will you sit down? And Uncle Worcester—
A plague upon it, I have forgot the map.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
Lord Mortimer and cousin Glendower,

A plague upon it, I have forgot the map.

sentences.speaker: GLENDOWER
sentences.text:
No, here it is. Sit, cousin Percy
Sit, good cousin Hotspur, for by that name
As oft as Lancaster doth speak of you
His cheek looks pale and with a rising sigh
He wisheth you in heaven.

sentences.speaker: GLENDOWER
sentences.text:
No, here it is. Sit, cousin Percy
Sit, good cousin Hotspur, for by that name

His cheek looks pale and with a rising sigh
He wisheth you in heaven.

sentences.speaker: GLENDOWER
sentences.text:
No, here it is. Sit, cousin Percy
Sit, good cousin Hotspur, for by that name
As oft as Lancaster doth speak of you
His cheek looks pale and with a rising sigh
He wisheth you in heaven.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
And you in hell,
As oft as he hears Owen Glendower spoke of.

sentences.speaker: GLENDOWER
sentences.text:
I cannot blame him. At my nativity
The front of heaven was full of fiery shapes,
Of burning cressets, and at my birth
The frame and huge foundation of the earth
Shaked like a coward.

sentences.speaker: GLENDOWER
sentences.text:
I cannot blame him. At my nativity
The front of heaven was full of fiery shapes,
Of burning cressets, and at my birth
The frame and huge foundation of the earth
Shaked like a coward.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
Why, so it would have done
At the same season if your mother’s cat
Had but kittened, though yourself had never been born.

sentences.speaker: GLENDOWER
sentences.text: I say the earth did shake when I was born.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
And I say the earth was not of my mind,
If you suppose as fearing you it shook.

sentences.speaker: GLENDOWER
sentences.text: The heavens were all on fire; the earth did tremble.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
O, then the earth shook to see the heavens on fire,
And not in fear of your nativity.
Diseas—d nature oftentimes breaks forth
In strange eruptions; oft the teeming earth
Is with a kind of colic pinched and vexed
By the imprisoning of unruly wind
Within her womb, which, for enlargement striving,
Shakes the old beldam earth and topples down
Steeples and moss-grown towers. At your birth
Our grandam earth, having this distemperature,
In passion shook.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:

And not in fear of your nativity.
Diseas—d nature oftentimes breaks forth
In strange eruptions; oft the teeming earth
Is with a kind of colic pinched and vexed
By the imprisoning of unruly wind
Within her womb, which, for enlargement striving,
Shakes the old beldam earth and topples down
Steeples and moss-grown towers. At your birth
Our grandam earth, having this distemperature,
In passion shook.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
O, then the earth shook to see the heavens on fire,
And not in fear of your nativity.
Diseas—d nature oftentimes breaks forth
In strange eruptions; oft the teeming earth

By the imprisoning of unruly wind
Within her womb, which, for enlargement striving,

Steeples and moss-grown towers. At your birth
Our grandam earth, having this distemperature,
In passion shook.

sentences.speaker: GLENDOWER
sentences.text:
Cousin, of many men
I do not bear these crossings. Give me leave
To tell you once again that at my birth
The front of heaven was full of fiery shapes,
The goats ran from the mountains, and the herds
Were strangely clamorous to the frighted fields.
These signs have marked me extraordinary,
And all the courses of my life do show
I am not in the roll of common men.
Where is he living, clipped in with the sea
That chides the banks of England, Scotland, Wales,
Which calls me pupil or hath read to me?
And bring him out that is but woman’s son
Can trace me in the tedious ways of art
And hold me pace in deep experiments.

sentences.text:
I think there’s no man speaks better Welsh.
I’ll to dinner.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
I think there’s no man speaks better Welsh.
I’ll to dinner.

sentences.speaker: MORTIMER
sentences.text: Peace, cousin Percy. You will make him mad.

sentences.speaker: MORTIMER
sentences.text: Peace, cousin Percy. You will make him mad.

sentences.speaker: GLENDOWER
sentences.text: I can call spirits from the vasty deep.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
Why, so can I, or so can any man,
But will they come when you do call for them?

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
Why, so can I, or so can any man,
But will they come when you do call for them?

sentences.speaker: GLENDOWER
sentences.text: Why, I can teach you, cousin, to command the devil.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
And I can teach thee, coz, to shame the devil
By telling truth. Tell truth and shame the devil.
If thou have power to raise him, bring him hither,
And I’ll be sworn I have power to shame him hence.
O, while you live, tell truth and shame the devil!

sentences.speaker: MORTIMER
sentences.text: Come, come, no more of this unprofitable chat.

sentences.speaker: GLENDOWER
sentences.text:
Three times hath Henry Bolingbroke made head
Against my power; thrice from the banks of Wye
And sandy-bottomed Severn have I sent him
Bootless home and weather-beaten back.

sentences.speaker: GLENDOWER
sentences.text:
Three times hath Henry Bolingbroke made head
Against my power; thrice from the banks of Wye
And sandy-bottomed Severn have I sent him
Bootless home and weather-beaten back.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
Home without boots, and in foul weather too!
How ’scapes he agues, in the devil’s name?

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
Home without boots, and in foul weather too!
How ’scapes he agues, in the devil’s name?

sentences.speaker: GLENDOWER
sentences.text:
Come, here’s the map. Shall we divide our right
According to our threefold order ta'en?

sentences.speaker: GLENDOWER
sentences.text:
Come, here’s the map. Shall we divide our right
According to our threefold order ta'en?

sentences.speaker: MORTIMER
sentences.text:
The Archdeacon hath divided it
Into three limits very equally:
England, from Trent and Severn hitherto,
By south and east is to my part assigned;
All westward, Wales beyond the Severn shore,
And all the fertile land within that bound
To Owen Glendower; and, dear coz, to you
The remnant northward, lying off from Trent.
And our indentures tripartite are drawn,
Which being sealèd interchangeably—
A business that this night may execute—
Tomorrow, cousin Percy, you and I
And my good Lord of Worcester will set forth
To meet your father and the Scottish power,
As is appointed us, at Shrewsbury.
My father Glendower is not ready yet,
Not shall we need his help these fourteen days.
(to GLENDOWER ) Within that space you may have drawn
together
Your tenants, friends, and neighboring gentlemen.

sentences.speaker: GLENDOWER
sentences.text:
A shorter time shall send me to you, lords,
And in my conduct shall your ladies come,
From whom you now must steal and take no leave,
For there will be a world of water shed
Upon the parting of your wives and you.

sentences.speaker: GLENDOWER
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
Methinks my moiety, north from Burton here,
In quantity equals not one of yours.
See how this river comes me cranking in
And cuts me from the best of all my land
A huge half-moon, a monstrous cantle out.
I’ll have the current in this place dammed up,
And here the smug and silver Trent shall run
In a new channel, fair and evenly.
It shall not wind with such a deep indent,
To rob me of so rich a bottom here.

sentences.speaker: GLENDOWER
sentences.text: Not wind? It shall, it must. You see it doth.

sentences.speaker: GLENDOWER
sentences.text: Not wind? It shall, it must. You see it doth.

sentences.speaker: GLENDOWER
sentences.text: Not wind? It shall, it must. You see it doth.

sentences.speaker: MORTIMER
sentences.text:
Yea, but
Mark how he bears his course, and runs me up
With like advantage on the other side,
Gelding the opposèd continent as much
As on the other side it takes from you.

sentences.speaker: WORCESTER
sentences.text:
Yea, but a little charge will trench him here
And on this north side win this cape of land,
And then he runs straight and even.

sentences.speaker: WORCESTER
sentences.text:
Yea, but a little charge will trench him here
And on this north side win this cape of land,
And then he runs straight and even.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text: I’ll have it so. A little charge will do it.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text: I’ll have it so. A little charge will do it.

sentences.speaker: GLENDOWER
sentences.text: I’ll not have it altered.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text: Will not you?

sentences.speaker: GLENDOWER
sentences.text: No, nor you shall not.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text: Who shall say me nay?

sentences.speaker: GLENDOWER
sentences.text: Why, that will I.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text: Let me not understand you, then; speak it in Welsh.

sentences.speaker: GLENDOWER
sentences.text:
I can speak English, lord, as well as you,
For I was trained up in the English court,
Where being but young I framèd to the harp
Many an English ditty lovely well
And gave the tongue a helpful ornament—
A virtue that was never seen in you.

sentences.speaker: GLENDOWER
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
Marry,
And I am glad of it with all my heart:
I had rather be a kitten and cry “mew”
Than one of these same meter balladmongers.
I had rather hear a brazen can’stick turned,
Or a dry wheel grate on the axletree,
And that would set my teeth nothing an edge,
Nothing so much as mincing poetry.
'Tis like the forced gait of a shuffling nag.

sentences.speaker: GLENDOWER
sentences.text: Come, you shall have Trent turned.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
I do not care. I’ll give thrice so much land
To any well-deserving friend;
But in the way of bargain, mark you me,
I’ll cavil on the ninth part of a hair.
Are the indentures drawn? Shall we be gone?

sentences.speaker: GLENDOWER
sentences.text:
The moon shines fair. You may away by night.
I’ll haste the writer, and withal
Break with your wives of your departure hence.
I am afraid my daughter will run mad,
So much she doteth on her Mortimer.

sentences.speaker: MORTIMER
sentences.text: Fie, cousin Percy, how you cross my father!

sentences.speaker: MORTIMER
sentences.text: Fie, cousin Percy, how you cross my father!

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
I cannot choose. Sometime he angers me
With telling me of the moldwarp and the ant,
Of the dreamer Merlin and his prophecies,
And of a dragon and a finless fish,
A clip-winged griffin and a moulten raven,
A couching lion and a ramping cat,
And such a deal of skimble-skamble stuff
As puts me from my faith. I tell you what—
He held me last night at least nine hours
In reckoning up the several devils' names
That were his lackeys. I cried “Hum,” and “Well, go
to,”
But marked him not a word. O, he is as tedious
As a tired horse, a railing wife,
Worse than a smoky house: I had rather live
With cheese and garlic in a windmill, far,
Than feed on cates and have him talk to me
In any summerhouse in Christendom.

In any summerhouse in Christendom.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
I cannot choose. Sometime he angers me
With telling me of the moldwarp and the ant,
Of the dreamer Merlin and his prophecies,
And of a dragon and a finless fish,
A clip-winged griffin and a moulten raven,
A couching lion and a ramping cat,
And such a deal of skimble-skamble stuff
As puts me from my faith. I tell you what—
He held me last night at least nine hours
In reckoning up the several devils' names
That were his lackeys. I cried “Hum,” and “Well, go
to,”
But marked him not a word. O, he is as tedious
As a tired horse, a railing wife,
Worse than a smoky house: I had rather live
With cheese and garlic in a windmill, far,
Than feed on cates and have him talk to me
In any summerhouse in Christendom.

sentences.speaker: MORTIMER
sentences.text:
In faith, he is a worthy gentleman,
Exceedingly well read and profited
In strange concealments, valiant as a lion,
And as wondrous affable, and as bountiful
As mines of India. Shall I tell you, cousin?
He holds your temper in a high respect
And curbs himself even of his natural scope
When you come cross his humor. Faith, he does.
I warrant you that man is not alive
Might so have tempted him as you have done
Without the taste of danger and reproof.
But do not use it oft, let me entreat you.

sentences.speaker: WORCESTER
sentences.text:
(to HOTSPUR ) In faith, my lord, you are too
willful-blame,
And, since your coming hither, have done enough
To put him quite beside his patience.
You must needs learn, lord, to amend this fault.
Though sometimes it show greatness, courage, blood—
And that’s the dearest grace it renders you—
Yet oftentimes it doth present harsh rage,
Defect of manners, want of government,
Pride, haughtiness, opinion, and disdain,
The least of which, haunting a nobleman,
Loseth men’s hearts and leaves behind a stain
Upon the beauty of all parts besides,
Beguiling them of commendation.

Here come our wives, and let us take our leave.

sentences.speaker: MORTIMER

This is the deadly spite that angers me:
My wife can speak no English, I no Welsh.

sentences.speaker: GLENDOWER
sentences.text:
My daughter weeps; she’ll not part with you.
She’ll be a soldier too, she’ll to the wars.

sentences.speaker: GLENDOWER

sentences.speaker: MORTIMER

Good father, tell her that she and my aunt Percy
Shall follow in your conduct speedily.

sentences.speaker: GLENDOWER

She is desperate here, a peevish self-willed harlotry,
One that no persuasion can do good upon.

sentences.speaker: MORTIMER
sentences.text:
I understand thy looks. That pretty Welsh
Which thou pourest down from these swelling heavens
I am too perfect in, and but for shame
In such a parley should I answer thee.

sentences.speaker: MORTIMER
sentences.text:
I understand thy kisses and thou mine,
And that’s a feeling disputation;
But I will never be a truant, love,
Till I have learned thy language; for thy tongue
Makes Welsh as sweet as ditties highly penned,
Sung by a fair queen in a summer’s bower,
With ravishing division, to her lute.

THE LADY speaks again in Welsh

sentences.speaker: MORTIMER
sentences.text: O, I am ignorance itself in this!

sentences.speaker: GLENDOWER
sentences.text:
She bids you on the wanton rushes lay you down
And rest your gentle head upon her lap,
And she will sing the song that pleaseth you
And on your eyelids crown the god of sleep,
Charming your blood with pleasing heaviness,
Making such difference ’twixt wake and sleep
As is the difference betwixt day and night
The hour before the heavenly harnessed team
Begins his golden progress in the east.

Here THE LADY sings a Welsh song

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text: Come, Kate, I’ll have your song too.

sentences.speaker: LADY PERCY
sentences.text: Not mine, in good sooth.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
Not yours, in good sooth! Heart, you swear like a
comfit-maker’s wife! “Not you, in good sooth,” and “as
true as I live,” and “as God shall mend me,” and “as
sure as day”—
And givest such sarcenet surety for thy oaths
As if thou never walk’st further than Finsbury.
Swear me, Kate, like a lady as thou art,
A good mouth-filling oath, and leave “in sooth,”
And such protest of pepper-gingerbread,
To velvet-guards and Sunday citizens.
Come, sing.

sentences.speaker: GLENDOWER
sentences.text:
Come, come, Lord Mortimer; you are as slow
As hot Lord Percy is on fire to go.

sentences.speaker: MORTIMER
sentences.text: With all my heart.

Enter KING, PRINCE HENRY of Wales, and others

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
Lords, give us leave; the Prince of Wales and I
Must have some private conference, but be near at hand,
For we shall presently have need of you.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
I know not whether God will have it so
For some displeasing service I have done,
That, in his secret doom, out of my blood
He’ll breed revengement and a scourge for me.
But thou dost in thy passages of life
Make me believe that thou art only marked
For the hot vengeance and the rod of heaven
To punish my mistreadings. Tell me else,
Could such inordinate and low desires,
Such poor, such bare, such lewd, such mean attempts,
Such barren pleasures, rude society
As thou art matched withal, and grafted to,
Accompany the greatness of thy blood,
And hold their level with thy princely heart?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
So please your Majesty, I would I could
Quit all offenses with as clear excuse
As well as I am doubtless I can purge
Myself of many I am charged withal.
Yet such extenuation let me beg
As, in reproof of many tales devised,
Which oft the ear of greatness needs must hear,
By smiling pickthanks and base newsmongers,
I may for some things true, wherein my youth
Hath faulty wandered and irregular,
Find pardon on my true submission.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
God pardon thee. Yet let me wonder, Harry,
At thy affections, which do hold a wing
Quite from the flight of all thy ancestors.
Thy place in council thou hast rudely lost,
Which by thy younger brother is supplied,
And art almost an alien to the hearts
Of all the court and princes of my blood.
The hope and expectation of thy time
Is ruined, and the soul of every man
Prophetically doth forethink thy fall.
Had I so lavish of my presence been,
So common-hackneyed in the eyes of men,
So stale and cheap to vulgar company,
Opinion, that did help me to the crown,
Had still kept loyal to possession
And left me in reputeless banishment,
A fellow of no mark nor likelihood.
By being seldom seen, I could not stir
But like a comet I was wondered at;
That men would tell their children “This is he.”
Others would say “Where? Which is Bolingbroke?”
And then I stole all courtesy from heaven,
And dressed myself in such humility
That I did pluck allegiance from men’s hearts,
Loud shouts and salutations from their mouths,
Even in the presence of the crownèd King.
Thus did I keep my person fresh and new,
My presence, like a robe pontifical,
Ne'er seen but wondered at, and so my state,
Seldom but sumptuous, showed like a feast
And won by rareness such solemnity.
The skipping King, he ambled up and down
With shallow jesters and rash bavin wits,
Soon kindled and soon burnt; carded his state,
Mingled his royalty with cap'ring fools,
Had his great name profanèd with their scorns,
And gave his countenance, against his name,
To laugh at gibing boys and stand the push
Of every beardless vain comparative;
Grew a companion to the common streets,
Enfeoffed himself to popularity,
That, being daily swallowed by men’s eyes,
They surfeited with honey and began
To loathe the taste of sweetness, whereof a little
More than a little is by much too much.
So, when he had occasion to be seen,
He was but as the cuckoo is in June,
Heard, not regarded; seen, but with such eyes
As, sick and blunted with community,
Afford no extraordinary gaze
Such as is bent on sunlike majesty
When it shines seldom in admiring eyes,
But rather drowsed and hung their eyelids down,
Slept in his face, and rendered such aspect
As cloudy men use to their adversaries,
Being with his presence glutted, gorged, and full.
And in that very line, Harry, standest thou,
For thou has lost thy princely privilege
With vile participation. Not an eye
But is aweary of thy common sight,
Save mine, which hath desired to see thee more,
Which now doth that I would not have it do,
Make blind itself with foolish tenderness.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
I shall hereafter, my thrice gracious lord,
Be more myself.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
For all the world
As thou art to this hour was Richard then
When I from France set foot at Ravenspurgh,
And even as I was then is Percy now.
Now, by my scepter, and my soul to boot,
He hath more worthy interest to the state
Than thou, the shadow of succession.
For of no right, nor color like to right,
He doth fill fields with harness in the realm,
Turns head against the lion’s armèd jaws,
And, being no more in debt to years than thou,
Leads ancient lords and reverend bishops on
To bloody battles and to bruising arms.
What never-dying honor hath he got
Against renownèd Douglas, whose high deeds,
Whose hot incursions and great name in arms,
Holds from all soldiers chief majority
And military title capital
Through all the kingdoms that acknowledge Christ.
Thrice hath this Hotspur, Mars in swathling clothes,
This infant warrior, in his enterprises
Discomfited great Douglas, ta'en him once,
Enlargèd him, and made a friend of him,
To fill the mouth of deep defiance up
And shake the peace and safety of our throne.
And what say you to this? Percy, Northumberland,
The Archbishop’s Grace of York, Douglas, Mortimer,
Capitulate against us and are up.
But wherefore do I tell these news to thee?
Why, Harry, do I tell thee of my foes,
Which art my nearest and dearest enemy?
Thou that art like enough, through vassal fear,
Base inclination, and the start of spleen,
To fight against me under Percy’s pay,
To dog his heels, and curtsy at his frowns,
To show how much thou art degenerate.

Which art my nearest and dearest enemy?
Thou that art like enough, through vassal fear,
Base inclination, and the start of spleen,
To fight against me under Percy’s pay,
To dog his heels, and curtsy at his frowns,
To show how much thou art degenerate.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
For all the world
As thou art to this hour was Richard then
When I from France set foot at Ravenspurgh,
And even as I was then is Percy now.
Now, by my scepter, and my soul to boot,
He hath more worthy interest to the state
Than thou, the shadow of succession.
For of no right, nor color like to right,
He doth fill fields with harness in the realm,
Turns head against the lion’s armèd jaws,
And, being no more in debt to years than thou,
Leads ancient lords and reverend bishops on
To bloody battles and to bruising arms.
What never-dying honor hath he got
Against renownèd Douglas, whose high deeds,
Whose hot incursions and great name in arms,
Holds from all soldiers chief majority
And military title capital
Through all the kingdoms that acknowledge Christ.
Thrice hath this Hotspur, Mars in swathling clothes,
This infant warrior, in his enterprises
Discomfited great Douglas, ta'en him once,
Enlargèd him, and made a friend of him,
To fill the mouth of deep defiance up
And shake the peace and safety of our throne.
And what say you to this? Percy, Northumberland,
The Archbishop’s Grace of York, Douglas, Mortimer,
Capitulate against us and are up.
But wherefore do I tell these news to thee?
Why, Harry, do I tell thee of my foes,

Thou that art like enough, through vassal fear,

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Do not think so. You shall not find it so.
And God forgive them that so much have swayed
Your Majesty’s good thoughts away from me.
I will redeem all this on Percy’s head,
And, in the closing of some glorious day,
Be bold to tell you that I am your son,
When I will wear a garment all of blood
And stain my favors in a bloody mask,
Which, washed away, shall scour my shame with it.
And that shall be the day, whene'er it lights,
That this same child of honor and renown,
This gallant Hotspur, this all-praisèd knight,
And your unthought-of Harry chance to meet.
For every honor sitting on his helm,
Would they were multitudes, and on my head
My shames redoubled! For the time will come
That I shall make this northern youth exchange
His glorious deeds for my indignities.
Percy is but my factor, good my lord,
To engross up glorious deeds on my behalf.
And I will call him to so strict account
That he shall render every glory up,
Yea, even the slightest worship of his time,
Or I will tear the reckoning from his heart.
This in the name of God I promise here,
The which if He be pleased I shall perform,
I do beseech your Majesty may salve
The long-grown wounds of my intemperance.
If not, the end of life cancels all bands,
And I will die a hundred thousand deaths
Ere break the smallest parcel of this vow.

I will redeem all this on Percy’s head,
And, in the closing of some glorious day,
Be bold to tell you that I am your son,
When I will wear a garment all of blood
And stain my favors in a bloody mask,
Which, washed away, shall scour my shame with it.
And that shall be the day, whene'er it lights,
That this same child of honor and renown,
This gallant Hotspur, this all-praisèd knight,
And your unthought-of Harry chance to meet.
For every honor sitting on his helm,
Would they were multitudes, and on my head
My shames redoubled! For the time will come
That I shall make this northern youth exchange
His glorious deeds for my indignities.
Percy is but my factor, good my lord,
To engross up glorious deeds on my behalf.
And I will call him to so strict account
That he shall render every glory up,
Yea, even the slightest worship of his time,
Or I will tear the reckoning from his heart.
This in the name of God I promise here,
The which if He be pleased I shall perform,
I do beseech your Majesty may salve
The long-grown wounds of my intemperance.
If not, the end of life cancels all bands,
And I will die a hundred thousand deaths
Ere break the smallest parcel of this vow.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
A hundred thousand rebels die in this.
Thou shalt have charge and sovereign trust herein.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
A hundred thousand rebels die in this.
Thou shalt have charge and sovereign trust herein.
Enter BLUNT

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text: How now, good Blunt? Thy looks are full of speed.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text: How now, good Blunt? Thy looks are full of speed.

sentences.speaker: BLUNT
sentences.text:
So hath the business that I come to speak of.
Lord Mortimer of Scotland hath sent word
That Douglas and the English rebels met
The eleventh of this month at Shrewsbury.
A mighty and a fearful head they are,
If promises be kept on every hand,
As ever offered foul play in the state.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
The Earl of Westmoreland set forth today,
With him my son, Lord John of Lancaster,
For this advertisement is five days old.—
On Wednesday next, Harry, you shall set forward.
On Thursday we ourselves will march. Our meeting
Is Bridgenorth. And, Harry, you shall march
Through Gloucestershire; by which account,
Our business valuèd, some twelve days hence
Our general forces at Bridgenorth shall meet.
Our hands are full of business. Let’s away.
Advantage feeds him fat while men delay.

Enter FALSTAFF and BARDOLPH

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Bardolph, am I not fallen away vilely since this last
action? Do I not bate? Do I not dwindle? Why, my skin
hangs about me like an like an old lady’s loose gown. I
am withered like an old applejohn. Well, I’ll repent,
and that suddenly, while I am in some liking. I shall be
out of heart shortly, and then I shall have no strength
to repent. An I have not forgotten what the inside of a
church is made of, I am a peppercorn, a brewer’s horse.
The inside of a church! Company, villanous company,
hath been the spoil of me.

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text: Sir John, you are so fretful you cannot live long.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Why, there is it. Come sing me a bawdy song, make me
merry. I was as virtuously given as a gentleman need to
be, virtuous enough: swore little; diced not above seven
times— a week; went to a bawdy house once in a
quarter—of an hour; paid money that I borrowed, three or
four times; lived well and in good compass; and now I
live out of all order, out of all compass.

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text:
Why, you are so fat, Sir John, that you must needs be
out of all compass, out of all reasonable compass, Sir
John.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Do thou amend thy face, and I’ll amend my life. Thou
art our admiral, thou bearest the lantern in the poop,
but ’tis in the nose of thee. Thou art the knight of the
burning lamp.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:

art our admiral, thou bearest the lantern in the poop,

burning lamp.

sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text: Why, Sir John, my face does you no harm.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
No, I’ll be sworn, I make as good use of it as many a
man doth of a death’s-head or a memento mori . I never
see thy face but I think upon hellfire and Dives that
lived in purple, for there he is in his robes, burning,
burning. If thou wert any way given to virtue, I would
swear by thy face. My oath should be “By this fire,
that’s God’s angel.” But thou art altogether given over,
and wert indeed, but for the light in thy face, the son
of utter darkness. When thou rannest up Gadshill in the
night to catch my horse, if I did not think thou hadst
been an ignis fatuus , or a ball of wildfire, there’s no
purchase in money. O, thou art a perpetual triumph, an
everlasting bonfire-light! Thou hast saved me a thousand
marks in links and torches, walking with thee in the
night betwixt tavern and tavern: but the sack that thou
hast drunk me would have bought me lights as good cheap
at the dearest chandler’s in Europe. I have maintained
that salamander of yours with fire any time this two and
thirty years, God reward me for it.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF

How now, Dame Partlet the hen, have you enquired yet
who picked my pocket?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
How now, Dame Partlet the hen, have you enquired yet

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
Why, Sir John, what do you think, Sir John, do you
think I keep thieves in my house? I have searched, I
have enquired, so has my husband, man by man, boy by
boy, servant by servant. The tithe of a hair was never
lost in my house before.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
[points at BARDOLPH ] He had his part of it. Let him
pay.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text: He? Alas, he is poor. He hath nothing.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text: He? Alas, he is poor. He hath nothing.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text: He? Alas, he is poor. He hath nothing.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
How, poor? Look upon his face. What call you rich? Let
them coin his nose. Let them coin his cheeks. I’ll not
pay a denier. What, will you make a younker of me? Shall
I not take mine case in mine inn but I shall have my
pocket picked? I have lost a seal ring of my
grandfather’s worth forty mark.

so.

you.

womanhood, Maid Marian may be the deputy’s wife of the
ward to thee. Go, you thing, go.

knighthood aside, thou art a knave to call me so.

sentences.text: What beast? Why, an otter.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: What beast? Why, an otter.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: An otter, Sir John. Why an otter?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: An otter, Sir John. Why an otter?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Why, she’s neither fish nor flesh; a man knows not
where to have her.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
Thou art an unjust man in saying so. Thou or any man
knows where to have me, thou knave, thou.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
Thou art an unjust man in saying so. Thou or any man
knows where to have me, thou knave, thou.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Thou sayest true, hostess, and he slanders thee most
grossly.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
So he doth you, my lord, and said this other day you
owed him a thousand pound.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Sirrah, do I owe you a thousand pound?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
A thousand pound, Hal? A million. Thy love is worth a
million; thou owest me thy love.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
A thousand pound, Hal? A million. Thy love is worth a
million; thou owest me thy love.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
A thousand pound, Hal? A million. Thy love is worth a
million; thou owest me thy love.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS QUICKLY
sentences.text:
Nay, my lord, he called you “jack,” and said he would
cudgel you.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Did I, Bardolph?

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text: Indeed, Sir John, you said so.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Yea, if he said my ring was copper.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
I say ’tis copper. Darest thou be as good as thy word
now?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
I say ’tis copper. Darest thou be as good as thy word
now?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Why, Hal, thou knowest, as thou art but man, I dare,
but as thou art Prince, I fear thee as I fear the
roaring of a lion’s whelp.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Why, Hal, thou knowest, as thou art but man, I dare,
but as thou art Prince, I fear thee as I fear the
roaring of a lion’s whelp.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: And why not as the lion?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
The King is to be feared as the lion. Dost thou think
I’ll fear thee as I fear thy father? Nay, an I do, I
pray God my girdle break.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
The King is to be feared as the lion. Dost thou think
I’ll fear thee as I fear thy father? Nay, an I do, I
pray God my girdle break.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
The King is to be feared as the lion. Dost thou think
I’ll fear thee as I fear thy father? Nay, an I do, I

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
O, if it should, how would thy guts fall about thy
knees! But, sirrah, there’s no room for faith, truth,
nor honesty in this bosom of thine. It is all filled up
with guts and midriff. Charge an honest woman with
picking thy pocket? Why, thou whoreson, impudent,
embossed rascal, if there were anything in thy pocket
but tavern reckonings, memorandums of bawdy houses, and
one poor pennyworth, of sugar candy to make thee
long-winded, if thy pocket were enriched with any other
injuries but these, I am a villain. And, yet you will
stand to it! You will not pocket up wrong! Art thou not
ashamed?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Dost thou hear, Hal? Thou knowest in the state of
innocency Adam fell, and what should poor Jack Falstaff
do in the days of villany? Thou seest I have more flesh
than another man and therefore more frailty. You
confess, then, you picked my pocket?

sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: It appears so by the story.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Hostess, I forgive thee. Go make ready breakfast, love
thy, husband, look to thy servants, cherish thy guests.
Thou shalt find me tractable to any honest reason. Thou
seest I am pacified still. Nay, prithee, be gone.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Now, Hal, to the news at court. For the robbery, lad,
how is that answered?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Now, Hal, to the news at court. For the robbery, lad,
how is that answered?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
O, my sweet beef, I must still be good angel to thee.
The money is paid back again.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
O, my sweet beef, I must still be good angel to thee.
The money is paid back again.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: O, I do not like that paying back. 'Tis a double labor.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: I am good friends with my father and may do anything.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Rob me the Exchequer the first thing thou dost, and do
it with unwashed hands too.

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text: Do, my lord.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: I have procured thee, Jack, a charge of foot.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
I would it had been of horse. Where shall I find one
that can steal well? O, for a fine thief of the age of
two and twenty or thereabouts! I am heinously
unprovided. Well, God be thanked for these rebels. They
offend none but the virtuous. I laud them; I praise
them.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Bardolph!

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text: My lord.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Go bear this letter to Lord John of Lancaster,
To my brother John; this to my Lord of Westmoreland.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Go bear this letter to Lord John of Lancaster,
To my brother John; this to my Lord of Westmoreland.
Exit BARDOLPH

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Go, Peto, to horse, to horse, for thou and I have
thirty miles to ride yet ere dinner time.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Go, Peto, to horse, to horse, for thou and I have
thirty miles to ride yet ere dinner time.
Exit PETO

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Jack, meet me tomorrow in the Temple hall
At two o'clock in the afternoon;
There shalt thou know thy charge, and there receive
Money and order for their furniture.
The land is burning. Percy stands on high,
And either we or they must lower lie.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Rare words, brave world!—Hostess, my breakfast, come.—
O, I could wish this tavern were my drum.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Rare words, brave world!—Hostess, my breakfast, come.—
O, I could wish this tavern were my drum.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:

O, I could wish this tavern were my drum.

Enter HOTSPUR, WORCESTER, and DOUGLAS

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
Well said, my noble Scot. If speaking truth
In this fine age were not thought flattery,
Such attribution should the Douglas have
As not a soldier of this season’s stamp
Should go so general current through the world.
By God, I cannot flatter. I do defy
The tongues of soothers. But a braver place
In my heart’s love hath no man than yourself.
Nay, task me to my word; approve me, lord.

Nay, task me to my word; approve me, lord.

sentences.speaker: DOUGLAS
sentences.text:
Thou art the king of honor.
No man so potent breathes upon the ground
But I will beard him.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text: Do so, and ’tis well.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text: Do so, and ’tis well.
Enter a MESSENGER with letters

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
What letters hast thou there? [To DOUGLAS] I can but
thank you.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: These letters come from your father.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text: Letters from him! Why comes he not himself?

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text: Letters from him! Why comes he not himself?

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: He cannot come, my lord. He is grievous sick.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
Zounds, how has he the leisure to be sick
In such a justling time? Who leads his power?
Under whose government come they along?

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
Zounds, how has he the leisure to be sick
In such a justling time? Who leads his power?
Under whose government come they along?

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
Zounds, how has he the leisure to be sick
In such a justling time? Who leads his power?
Under whose government come they along?

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: His letters bear his mind, not I, my lord.

sentences.speaker: WORCESTER
sentences.text: I prithee, tell me, doth he keep his bed?

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
He did, my lord, four days ere I set forth,
And, at the time of my departure thence,
He was much feared by his physicians.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
He did, my lord, four days ere I set forth,
And, at the time of my departure thence,
He was much feared by his physicians.

sentences.speaker: WORCESTER
sentences.text:
I would the state of time had first been whole
Ere he by sickness had been visited.
His health was never better worth than now.

sentences.speaker: WORCESTER
sentences.text:
I would the state of time had first been whole
Ere he by sickness had been visited.
His health was never better worth than now.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
Sick now? Droop now? This sickness doth infect
The very lifeblood of our enterprise.
'Tis catching hither, even to our camp.
He writes me here that inward sickness—
And that his friends by deputation
Could not so soon be drawn, nor did he think it meet
To lay so dangerous and dear a trust
On any soul removed but on his own;
Yet doth he give us bold advertisement
That with our small conjunction we should on
To see how fortune is disposed to us,
For, as he writes, there is no quailing now,
Because the King is certainly possessed
Of all our purposes. What say you to it?

For, as he writes, there is no quailing now,
Because the King is certainly possessed
Of all our purposes. What say you to it?

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
Sick now? Droop now? This sickness doth infect
The very lifeblood of our enterprise.
'Tis catching hither, even to our camp.
He writes me here that inward sickness—
And that his friends by deputation
Could not so soon be drawn, nor did he think it meet
To lay so dangerous and dear a trust
On any soul removed but on his own;
Yet doth he give us bold advertisement
That with our small conjunction we should on
To see how fortune is disposed to us,
For, as he writes, there is no quailing now,
Because the King is certainly possessed
Of all our purposes. What say you to it?

sentences.speaker: WORCESTER
sentences.text: Your father’s sickness is a maim to us.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
A perilous gash, a very limb lopped off!
And yet, in faith, it is not. His present want
Seems more than we shall find it. Were it good
To set the exact wealth of all our states
All at one cast? To set so rich a main
On the nice hazard of one doubtful hour?
It were not good, for therein should we read
The very bottom and the soul of hope,
The very list, the very utmost bound
Of all our fortunes.

sentences.speaker: DOUGLAS
sentences.text:
Faith, and so we should, where now remains
A sweet reversion. We may boldly spend
Upon the hope of what is to come in.
A comfort of retirement lives in this.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
A rendezvous, a home to fly unto,
If that the devil and mischance look big
Upon the maidenhead of our affairs.

sentences.speaker: WORCESTER
sentences.text:
But yet I would your father had been here.
The quality and hair of our attempt
Brooks no division. It will be thought
By some that know not why he is away
That wisdom, loyalty, and mere dislike
Of our proceedings kept the Earl from hence.
And think how such an apprehension
May turn the tide of fearful faction
And breed a kind of question in our cause.
For well you know, we of the off'ring side
Must keep aloof from strict arbitrament,
And stop all sight-holes, every loop from whence
The eye of reason may pry in upon us.
This absence of your father’s draws a curtain
That shows the ignorant a kind of fear
Before not dreamt of.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
You strain too far.
I rather of his absence make this use:
It lends a luster and more great opinion,
A larger dare, to our great enterprise
Than if the Earl were here, for men must think
If we without his help can make a head
To push against a kingdom, with his help
We shall o'erturn it topsy-turvy down.
Yet all goes well, yet all our joints are whole.

As heart can think. There is not such a word
Spoke of in Scotland as this term of fear.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text: My cousin Vernon, welcome, by my soul.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text: My cousin Vernon, welcome, by my soul.

sentences.speaker: VERNON
sentences.text:
Pray God my news be worth a welcome, lord.
The Earl of Westmoreland, seven thousand strong,
Is marching hitherwards, with him Prince John.

sentences.speaker: VERNON
sentences.text:
Pray God my news be worth a welcome, lord.
The Earl of Westmoreland, seven thousand strong,
Is marching hitherwards, with him Prince John.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text: No harm, what more?

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text: No harm, what more?

sentences.speaker: VERNON
sentences.text:
And further I have learned,
The King himself in person is set forth,
Or hitherwards intended speedily,
With strong and mighty preparation.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
He shall be welcome too. Where is his son,
The nimble-footed madcap Prince of Wales,
And his comrades, that daffed the world aside
And bid it pass?

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
He shall be welcome too. Where is his son,
The nimble-footed madcap Prince of Wales,
And his comrades, that daffed the world aside
And bid it pass?

sentences.speaker: VERNON
sentences.text:
All furnished, all in arms,
All plumed like ostriches that with the wind
Baited like eagles having lately bathed,
Glittering in golden coats like images,
As full of spirit as the month of May,
And gorgeous as the sun at midsummer,
Wanton as youthful goats, wild as young bulls.
I saw young Harry with his beaver on,
His cuisses on his thighs, gallantly armed
Rise from the ground like feathered Mercury
And vaulted with such ease into his seat
As if an angel dropped down from the clouds,
To turn and wind a fiery Pegasus
And witch the world with noble horsemanship.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
No more, no more! Worse than the sun in March
This praise doth nourish agues. Let them come.
They come like sacrifices in their trim,
And to the fire-eyed maid of smoky war
All hot and bleeding will we offer them.
The mailèd Mars shall on his altar sit
Up to the ears in blood. I am on fire
To hear this rich reprisal is so nigh
And yet not ours. Come, let me taste my horse,
Who is to bear me like a thunderbolt
Against the bosom of the Prince of Wales.
Harry to Harry shall, hot horse to horse,
Meet and ne'er part till one drop down a corpse.
O, that Glendower were come!

sentences.speaker: VERNON
sentences.text:
There is more news.
I learned in Worcester, as I rode along,
He cannot draw his power this fourteen days.

sentences.speaker: DOUGLAS
sentences.text: That’s the worst tidings that I hear of yet.

sentences.speaker: WORCESTER
sentences.text: Ay, by my faith, that bears a frosty sound.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text: What may the King’s whole battle reach unto?

sentences.speaker: VERNON
sentences.text: To thirty thousand.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
Forty let it be.
My father and Glendower being both away,
The powers of us may serve so great a day.
Come, let us take a muster speedily.
Doomsday is near. Die all, die merrily.
