sentences.text:
Forty let it be.
My father and Glendower being both away,
The powers of us may serve so great a day.
Come, let us take a muster speedily.
Doomsday is near. Die all, die merrily.

sentences.speaker: DOUGLAS
sentences.text:
Talk not of dying. I am out of fear
Of death or death’s hand for this one half year.

sentences.speaker: DOUGLAS
sentences.text:
Talk not of dying. I am out of fear
Of death or death’s hand for this one half year.
Exeunt
Enter FALSTAFF and BARDOLPH

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Bardolph, get thee before to Coventry. Fill me a bottle
of sack. Our soldiers shall march through. We’ll to
Sutton Coldfield tonight.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Bardolph, get thee before to Coventry. Fill me a bottle
of sack. Our soldiers shall march through. We’ll to
Sutton Coldfield tonight.

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text: Will you give me money, captain?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Lay out, lay out.

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text: This bottle makes an angel.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
An if it do, take it for thy labor. An if it make
twenty, take them all. I’ll answer the coinage. Bid my
lieutenant Peto meet me at town’s end.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
An if it do, take it for thy labor. An if it make
twenty, take them all. I’ll answer the coinage. Bid my
lieutenant Peto meet me at town’s end.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
An if it do, take it for thy labor. An if it make
twenty, take them all. I’ll answer the coinage. Bid my
lieutenant Peto meet me at town’s end.

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text: I will, captain. Farewell.

sentences.speaker: BARDOLPH
sentences.text: I will, captain. Farewell.
Exit BARDOLPH

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
If I be not ashamed of my soldiers, I am a soused
gurnet. I have misused the King’s press damnably. I have
got, in exchange of a hundred and fifty soldiers, three
hundred and odd pounds. I press me none but good
householders, yeomen’s sons; inquire me out contracted
bachelors, such as had been asked twice on the banns;
such a commodity of warm slaves—as had as lief hear the
devil as a drum, such as fear the report of a caliver
worse than a struck fowl or a hurt wild duck. I pressed
me none but such toasts-and-butter, with hearts in their
bellies no bigger than pins' heads, and they have
bought out their services, and now my whole charge
consists of ancients, corporals, lieutenants, gentlemen
of companies— slaves as ragged as Lazarus in the painted
cloth, where the glutton’s dogs licked his sores; and
such as indeed were never soldiers, but discarded,
unjust servingmen, younger sons to younger brothers,
revolted tapsters, and ostlers tradefallen, the cankers
of a calm world and a long peace, ten times more
dishonorable-ragged than an old feazed ancient; and such
have I to fill up the rooms of them that have bought
out their services, that you would think that I had a
hundred and fifty tattered prodigals lately come from
swine-keeping, from eating draff and husks. A mad fellow
met me on the way and told me I had unloaded all the
gibbets and pressed the dead bodies. No eye hath seen
such scarecrows. I’ll not march through Coventry with
them, that’s flat. Nay, and the villains march wide
betwixt the legs as if they had gyves on, for indeed I
had the most of them out of prison. There’s not a shirt
and a half in all my company, and the half shirt is two
napkins tacked together and thrown over the shoulders
like a herald’s coat without sleeves; and the shirt, to
say the truth, stolen from my host at Saint Albans or
the red-nose innkeeper of Daventry. But that’s all one;
they’ll find linen enough on every hedge.

and a half in all my company, and the half shirt is two
napkins tacked together and thrown over the shoulders
like a herald’s coat without sleeves; and the shirt, to
say the truth, stolen from my host at Saint Albans or
the red-nose innkeeper of Daventry. But that’s all one;
they’ll find linen enough on every hedge.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
If I be not ashamed of my soldiers, I am a soused
gurnet. I have misused the King’s press damnably. I have
got, in exchange of a hundred and fifty soldiers, three
hundred and odd pounds. I press me none but good
householders, yeomen’s sons; inquire me out contracted
bachelors, such as had been asked twice on the banns;
such a commodity of warm slaves—as had as lief hear the
devil as a drum, such as fear the report of a caliver
worse than a struck fowl or a hurt wild duck. I pressed
me none but such toasts-and-butter, with hearts in their
bellies no bigger than pins' heads, and they have
bought out their services, and now my whole charge
consists of ancients, corporals, lieutenants, gentlemen
of companies— slaves as ragged as Lazarus in the painted
cloth, where the glutton’s dogs licked his sores; and
such as indeed were never soldiers, but discarded,
unjust servingmen, younger sons to younger brothers,
revolted tapsters, and ostlers tradefallen, the cankers
of a calm world and a long peace, ten times more
dishonorable-ragged than an old feazed ancient; and such
have I to fill up the rooms of them that have bought
out their services, that you would think that I had a
hundred and fifty tattered prodigals lately come from
swine-keeping, from eating draff and husks. A mad fellow
met me on the way and told me I had unloaded all the
gibbets and pressed the dead bodies. No eye hath seen
such scarecrows. I’ll not march through Coventry with
them, that’s flat. Nay, and the villains march wide
betwixt the legs as if they had gyves on, for indeed I
had the most of them out of prison. There’s not a shirt
and a half in all my company, and the half shirt is two
napkins tacked together and thrown over the shoulders
like a herald’s coat without sleeves; and the shirt, to
say the truth, stolen from my host at Saint Albans or
the red-nose innkeeper of Daventry. But that’s all one;
they’ll find linen enough on every hedge.

Enter PRINCE HENRY and Lord WESTMORELAND

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: How now, blown Jack? How now, quilt?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: How now, blown Jack? How now, quilt?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
What, Hal, how now, mad wag? What a devil dost thou in
Warwickshire?—My good Lord of Westmoreland, I cry you
mercy: I thought your Honor had already been at
Shrewsbury.

sentences.speaker: WESTMORELAND
sentences.text:
Faith, Sir John, ’tis more than time that I were there
and you too, but my powers are there already. The King,
I can tell you, looks for us all. We must away all
night.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Tut, never fear me. I am as vigilant as a cat to steal
cream.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Tut, never fear me. I am as vigilant as a cat to steal
cream.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
I think to steal cream indeed, for thy theft hath
already made thee butter. But tell me, Jack, whose
fellows are these that come after?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:

fellows are these that come after?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Mine, Hal, mine.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: I did never see such pitiful rascals.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
FALSTAFF
FALSTAFF
WESTMORELAND
sentences.text:
Ay, but, Sir John, methinks they are exceeding poor and
bare, too beggarly.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Faith, for their poverty, I know not where they had
that, and for their bareness, I am sure they never
learned that of me.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
No, I’ll be sworn, unless you call three fingers in the
ribs bare. But, sirrah, make haste. Percy is already in
the field.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: What, is the King encamped?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: What, is the King encamped?

sentences.speaker: WESTMORELAND
sentences.text: He is, Sir John. I fear we shall stay too long.

sentences.speaker: WESTMORELAND
sentences.text: He is, Sir John. I fear we shall stay too long.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Well,
To the latter end of a fray and the beginning of a
feast
Fits a dull fighter and a keen guest.
Exeunt
Enter HOTSPUR, WORCESTER, DOUGLAS, and VERNON

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text: We’ll fight with him tonight.

sentences.speaker: WORCESTER
sentences.text: It may not be.

sentences.speaker: DOUGLAS
sentences.text: You give him then advantage.

sentences.speaker: VERNON
sentences.text: Not a whit.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text: Why say you so? Looks he not for supply?

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text: Why say you so? Looks he not for supply?

sentences.speaker: VERNON
sentences.text: So do we.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text: His is certain; ours is doubtful.

sentences.speaker: WORCESTER
sentences.text: Good cousin, be advised. Stir not tonight.

sentences.speaker: WORCESTER
sentences.text: Good cousin, be advised. Stir not tonight.

sentences.speaker: VERNON
sentences.text: [To HOTSPUR] Do not, my lord.

sentences.speaker: DOUGLAS
sentences.text:
You do not counsel well.
You speak it out of fear and cold heart.

sentences.speaker: DOUGLAS
sentences.text:
You do not counsel well.
You speak it out of fear and cold heart.

sentences.speaker: VERNON
sentences.text:
Do me no slander, Douglas. By my life
(And I dare well maintain it with my life),
If well-respected honor bid me on,
I hold as little counsel with weak fear
As you, my lord, or any Scot that this day lives.
Let it be seen tomorrow in the battle
Which of us fears.

sentences.speaker: DOUGLAS
sentences.text: Yea, or tonight.

sentences.speaker: VERNON
sentences.text: Content.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text: Tonight, say I.

sentences.speaker: VERNON
sentences.text:
Come, come it nay not be. I wonder much,
Being men of such great leading as you are,
That you foresee not what impediments
Drag back our expedition. Certain horse
Of my cousin Vernon’s are not yet come up.
Your Uncle Worcester’s horse came but today,
And now their pride and mettle is asleep,
Their courage with hard labor tame and dull,
That not a horse is half the half of himself.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
So are the horses of the enemy
In general journey-bated and brought low.
The better part of ours are full of rest.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
So are the horses of the enemy
In general journey-bated and brought low.
The better part of ours are full of rest.

sentences.speaker: WORCESTER
sentences.text:
The number of the King exceedeth ours.
For God’s sake, cousin, stay till all come in.

sentences.speaker: WORCESTER
sentences.text:
The number of the King exceedeth ours.
For God’s sake, cousin, stay till all come in.
The trumpet sounds a parley
The trumpet sounds a parley
Enter BLUNT

sentences.speaker: BLUNT
sentences.text:
I come with gracious offers from the King,
If you vouchsafe me hearing and respect.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
Welcome, Sir Walter Blunt, and would to God
You were of our determination.
Some of us love you well, and even those some
Envy your great deservings and good name
Because you are not of our quality
But stand against us like an enemy.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
Welcome, Sir Walter Blunt, and would to God
You were of our determination.
Some of us love you well, and even those some
Envy your great deservings and good name
Because you are not of our quality
But stand against us like an enemy.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
Welcome, Sir Walter Blunt, and would to God
You were of our determination.
Some of us love you well, and even those some
Envy your great deservings and good name
Because you are not of our quality
But stand against us like an enemy.

sentences.speaker: BLUNT
sentences.text:
And God defend but still I should stand so,
So long as out of limit and true rule
You stand against anointed majesty.
But to my charge. The king hath sent to know
The nature of your griefs, and whereupon
You conjure from the breast of civil peace
Such bold hostility, teaching his duteous land
Audacious cruelty. If that the king
Have any way your good deserts forgot,
Which he confesseth to be manifold,
He bids you name your griefs, and with all speed
You shall have your desires with interest
And pardon absolute for yourself and these
Herein misled by your suggestion.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
The King is kind, and well we know the king
Knows at what time to promise, when to pay.
My father and my uncle and myself
Did give him that same royalty he wears,
And when he was not six-and-twenty strong,
Sick in the world’s regard, wretched and low,
A poor unminded outlaw sneaking home,
My father gave him welcome to the shore;
And when he heard him swear and vow to God
He came but to be Duke of Lancaster,
To sue his livery, and beg his peace,
With tears of innocency and terms of zeal,
My father, in kind heart and pity moved,
Swore him assistance and performed it too.
Now when the lords and barons of the realm
Perceived Northumberland did lean to him,
The more and less came in with cap and knee,
Met him in boroughs, cities, villages,
Attended him on bridges, stood in lanes,
Laid gifts before him, proffered him their oaths,
Gave him their heirs as pages, followed him
Even at the heels in golden multitudes.
He presently, as greatness knows itself,
Steps me a little higher than his vow
Made to my father while his blood was poor
Upon the naked shore at Ravenspurgh,
And now forsooth takes on him to reform
Some certain edicts and some strait decrees
That lie too heavy on the commonwealth,
Cries out upon abuses, seems to weep
Over his country’s wrongs, and by this face,
This seeming brow of justice, did he win
The hearts of all that he did angle for,
Proceeded further—cut me off the heads
Of all the favourites that the absent King
In deputation left behind him here
When he was personal in the Irish war.

Gave him their heirs as pages, followed him
Even at the heels in golden multitudes.
He presently, as greatness knows itself,
Steps me a little higher than his vow
Made to my father while his blood was poor
Upon the naked shore at Ravenspurgh,
And now forsooth takes on him to reform
Some certain edicts and some strait decrees
That lie too heavy on the commonwealth,
Cries out upon abuses, seems to weep
Over his country’s wrongs, and by this face,
This seeming brow of justice, did he win
The hearts of all that he did angle for,
Proceeded further—cut me off the heads
Of all the favourites that the absent King
In deputation left behind him here
When he was personal in the Irish war.

sentences.speaker: BLUNT
sentences.text: Tut, I came not to hear this.

sentences.speaker: BLUNT
sentences.text: Tut, I came not to hear this.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
Then to the point.
In short time after, he deposed the King,
Soon after that deprived him of his life
And, in the neck of that, tasked the whole state.
To make that worse, suffered his kinsman March
(Who is, if every owner were well placed,
Indeed his king) to be engaged in Wales,
There without ransom to lie forfeited,
Disgraced me in my happy victories,
Sought to entrap me by intelligence,
Rated mine uncle from the council board,
In rage dismissed my father from the court,
Broke oath on oath, committed wrong on wrong,
And in conclusion drove us to seek out
This head of safety, and withal to pry
Into his title, the which we find
Too indirect for long continuance.

sentences.speaker: BLUNT
sentences.text: Shall I return this answer to the King?

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
Not so, Sir Walter. We’ll withdraw awhile.
Go to the King, and let there be impawned
Some surety for a safe return again,
And in the morning early shall my uncle
Bring him our purposes. And so farewell.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR

sentences.speaker: BLUNT
sentences.text: I would you would accept of grace and love.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text: And maybe so we shall.

sentences.speaker: BLUNT
sentences.text: Pray God you do.
Exeunt
Enter the ARCHBISHOP of York and SIR MICHAEL

sentences.speaker: ARCHBISHOP
sentences.text:
Hie, good Sir Michael, bear this sealèd brief
With winged haste to the Lord Marshal,
This to my cousin Scrope, and all the rest
To whom they are directed. If you knew
How much they do import, you would make haste.

sentences.speaker: ARCHBISHOP
sentences.text:
Hie, good Sir Michael, bear this sealèd brief
With winged haste to the Lord Marshal,
This to my cousin Scrope, and all the rest
To whom they are directed. If you knew
How much they do import, you would make haste.

sentences.speaker: SIR MICHAEL
sentences.text: My good lord, I guess their tenor.

sentences.speaker: ARCHBISHOP
sentences.text:
Like enough you do.
Tomorrow, good Sir Michael, is a day
Wherein the fortune of ten thousand men
Must bide the touch. For, sir, at Shrewsbury,
As I am truly given to understand,
The King with mighty and quick-raisèd power
Meets with Lord Harry. And I fear, Sir Michael,
What with the sickness of Northumberland,
Whose power was in the first proportion,
And what with Owen Glendower’s absence thence,
Who with them was a rated sinew too
And comes not in, o'er-ruled by prophecies,
I fear the power of Percy is too weak
To wage an instant trial with the King.

Must bide the touch. For, sir, at Shrewsbury,
As I am truly given to understand,
The King with mighty and quick-raisèd power
Meets with Lord Harry. And I fear, Sir Michael,
What with the sickness of Northumberland,
Whose power was in the first proportion,
And what with Owen Glendower’s absence thence,
Who with them was a rated sinew too
And comes not in, o'er-ruled by prophecies,
I fear the power of Percy is too weak
To wage an instant trial with the King.

sentences.speaker: SIR MICHAEL
sentences.text:
Why, my good lord, you need not fear.
There is Douglas and Lord Mortimer.

sentences.speaker: SIR MICHAEL
sentences.text:
Why, my good lord, you need not fear.
There is Douglas and Lord Mortimer.

sentences.speaker: ARCHBISHOP
sentences.text: No, Mortimer is not there.

sentences.speaker: SIR MICHAEL
sentences.text:
But there is Mordake, Vernon, Lord Harry Percy,
And there is my Lord of Worcester and a head
Of gallant warriors, noble gentlemen.

sentences.speaker: SIR MICHAEL
sentences.text:
But there is Mordake, Vernon, Lord Harry Percy,
And there is my Lord of Worcester and a head
Of gallant warriors, noble gentlemen.

sentences.speaker: ARCHBISHOP
sentences.text:
And so there is. But yet the King hath drawn
The special head of all the land together:
The Prince of Wales, Lord John of Lancaster,
The noble Westmoreland, and warlike Blunt,
And many more corrivals and dear men
Of estimation and command in arms.

sentences.speaker: ARCHBISHOP
sentences.text:
And so there is. But yet the King hath drawn
The special head of all the land together:
The Prince of Wales, Lord John of Lancaster,
The noble Westmoreland, and warlike Blunt,
And many more corrivals and dear men
Of estimation and command in arms.

sentences.speaker: SIR MICHAEL
sentences.text: Doubt not, my lord, they shall be well opposed.

sentences.speaker: ARCHBISHOP
sentences.text:
I hope no less, yet needful ’tis to fear;
And to prevent the worst, Sir Michael, speed.
For if Lord Percy thrive not, ere the King
Dismiss his power, he means to visit us,
For he hath heard of our confederacy,
And ’tis but wisdom to make strong against him:
Therefore make haste. I must go write again
To other friends. And so farewell, Sir Michael.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
How bloodily the sun begins to peer
Above yon busky hill. The day looks pale
At his distemp'rature.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
How bloodily the sun begins to peer
Above yon busky hill. The day looks pale
At his distemp'rature.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
The southern wind
Doth play the trumpet to his purposes,
And by his hollow whistling in the leaves
Foretells a tempest and a blust'ring day.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
The southern wind
Doth play the trumpet to his purposes,
And by his hollow whistling in the leaves
Foretells a tempest and a blust'ring day.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
Then with the losers let it sympathize,
For nothing can seem foul to those that win.
The trumpet sounds. Enter WORCESTER and VERNON
The trumpet sounds. Enter WORCESTER and VERNON

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
How now, my Lord of Worcester? ' Tis not well
That you and I should meet upon such terms
As now we meet. You have deceived our trust
And made us doff our easy robes of peace
To crush our old limbs in ungentle steel.
This is not well, my lord; this is not well.
What say you to it? Will you again unknit
This churlish knot of all-abhorrèd war
And move in that obedient orb again
Where you did give a fair and natural light,
And be no more an exhaled meteor,
A prodigy of fear and a portent
Of broachèd mischief to the unborn times?

sentences.speaker: WORCESTER
sentences.text:
Hear me, my liege:
For mine own part I could be well content
To entertain the lag end of my life
With quiet hours. For I do protest
I have not sought the day of this dislike.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text: You have not sought it? How comes it then?

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text: You have not sought it? How comes it then?

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: Rebellion lay in his way, and he found it.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Peace, chewet, peace.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Peace, chewet, peace.

sentences.speaker: WORCESTER
sentences.text:
(to the KING ) It pleased your Majesty to turn your
looks
Of favour from myself and all our house;
And yet I must remember you, my lord,
We were the first and dearest of your friends.
For you my staff of office did I break
In Richard’s time, and posted day and night
To meet you on the way, and kiss your hand
When yet you were in place and in account
Nothing so strong and fortunate as I.
It was myself, my brother, and his son
That brought you home and boldly did outdare
The dangers of the time. You swore to us,
And you did swear that oath at Doncaster,
That you did nothing purpose 'gainst the state,
Nor claim no further than your new-fall'n right,
The seat of Gaunt, dukedom of Lancaster.
To this we swore our aid. But in short space
It rained down fortune show'ring on your head,
And such a flood of greatness fell on you—
What with our help, what with the absent King,
What with the injuries of a wanton time,
The seeming sufferances that you had borne,
And the contrarious winds that held the King
So long in his unlucky Irish wars
That all in England did repute him dead—
And from this swarm of fair advantages
You took occasion to be quickly wooed
To gripe the general sway into your hand,
Forget your oath to us at Doncaster;
And being fed by us, you used us so
As that ungentle gull, the cuckoo’s bird,
Useth the sparrow—did oppress our nest,
Grew by our feeding to so great a bulk
That even our love durst not come near your sight
For fear of swallowing; but with nimble wing
We were enforced for safety sake to fly
Out of sight and raise this present head,
Whereby we stand opposèd by such means
As you yourself have forged against yourself
By unkind usage, dangerous countenance,
And violation of all faith and troth
Sworn to us in your younger enterprise.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
These things indeed you have articulate,
Proclaimed at market crosses, read in churches,
To face the garment of rebellion
With some fine color that may please the eye
Of fickle changelings and poor discontents,
Which gape and rub the elbow at the news
Of hurlyburly innovation.
And never yet did insurrection want
Such water colors to impaint his cause,
Nor moody beggars starving for a time
Of pellmell havoc and confusion.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
In both your armies there is many a soul
Shall pay full dearly for this encounter
If once they join in trial. Tell your nephew,
The Prince of Wales doth join with all the world
In praise of Henry Percy. By my hopes,
This present enterprise set off his head,
I do not think a braver gentleman,
More active-valiant, or more valiant-young,
More daring or more bold, is now alive
To grace this latter age with noble deeds.
For my part, I may speak it to my shame,
I have a truant been to chivalry,
And so I hear he doth account me too.
Yet this before my father’s majesty:
I am content that he shall take the odds
Of his great name and estimation,
And will, to save the blood on either side,
Try fortune with him in a single fight.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
And, Prince of Wales, so dare we venture thee,
Albeit considerations infinite
Do make against it.— No, good Worcester, no,
We love our people well, even those we love
That are misled upon your cousin’s part.
And, will they take the offer of our grace,
Both he and they and you, yea, every man
Shall be my friend again, and I’ll be his.
So tell your cousin, and bring me word
What he will do. But if he will not yield,
Rebuke and dread correction wait on us,
And they shall do their office. So begone.
We will not now be troubled with reply.
We offer fair. Take it advisedly.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
It will not be accepted, on my life.
The Douglas and the Hotspur both together
Are confident against the world in arms.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
Hence, therefore, every leader to his charge,
For on their answer will we set on them,
And God befriend us as our cause is just.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
Hence, therefore, every leader to his charge,
For on their answer will we set on them,
And God befriend us as our cause is just.
Exeunt all but PRINCE HENRY and FALSTAFF

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
FALSTAFF
PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Nothing but a colossus can do thee that friendship.
Say thy prayers, and farewell.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Nothing but a colossus can do thee that friendship.
Say thy prayers, and farewell.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: I would ’twere bedtime, Hal, and all well.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Why, thou owest God a death.
Exit PRINCE HENRY

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
'Tis not due yet. I would be loath to pay Him before
His day. What need I be so forward with Him that calls
not on me? Well, ’tis no matter. Honour pricks me on.
Yea, but how if honor prick me off when I come on? How
then? Can honor set to a leg? no. Or an arm? no. Or take
away the grief of a wound? No. Honor hath no skill in
surgery, then? No. What is honor? A word. What is in
that word “honor”? What is that “honor”? Air. A trim
reckoning. Who hath it? He that died o' Wednesday. Doth
he feel it? No. Doth he hear it? No. 'Tis insensible,
then? Yea, to the dead. But will it not live with the
living? No. Why? Detraction will not suffer it.
Therefore, I’ll none of it. Honor is a mere scutcheon.
And so ends my catechism.

that word “honor”? What is that “honor”? Air. A trim
reckoning. Who hath it? He that died o' Wednesday. Doth
he feel it? No. Doth he hear it? No. 'Tis insensible,
then? Yea, to the dead. But will it not live with the
living? No. Why? Detraction will not suffer it.
Therefore, I’ll none of it. Honor is a mere scutcheon.
And so ends my catechism.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
'Tis not due yet. I would be loath to pay Him before
His day. What need I be so forward with Him that calls
not on me? Well, ’tis no matter. Honour pricks me on.
Yea, but how if honor prick me off when I come on? How
then? Can honor set to a leg? no. Or an arm? no. Or take
away the grief of a wound? No. Honor hath no skill in
surgery, then? No. What is honor? A word. What is in
that word “honor”? What is that “honor”? Air. A trim
reckoning. Who hath it? He that died o' Wednesday. Doth
he feel it? No. Doth he hear it? No. 'Tis insensible,
then? Yea, to the dead. But will it not live with the
living? No. Why? Detraction will not suffer it.
Therefore, I’ll none of it. Honor is a mere scutcheon.
And so ends my catechism.

Enter WORCESTER and Sir Richard VERNON

sentences.speaker: WORCESTER
sentences.text:
O no, my nephew must not know, Sir Richard,
The liberal and kind offer of the King.

sentences.speaker: WORCESTER
sentences.text:
O no, my nephew must not know, Sir Richard,
The liberal and kind offer of the King.

sentences.speaker: VERNON
sentences.text: 'Twere best he did.

sentences.speaker: WORCESTER
sentences.text:
Then are we all undone.
It is not possible, it cannot be
The King should keep his word in loving us.
He will suspect us still and find a time
To punish this offense in other faults.
Suspicion all our lives shall be stuck full of eyes,
For treason is but trusted like the fox,
Who, never so tame, so cherished and locked up,
Will have a wild trick of his ancestors.
Look how we can, or sad or merrily,
Interpretation will misquote our looks,
And we shall feed like oxen at a stall,
The better cherished still the nearer death.
My nephew’s trespass may be well forgot;
It hath the excuse of youth and heat of blood,
And an adopted name of privilege—
A hairbrained Hotspur governed by a spleen:
All his offenses live upon my head
And on his father’s. We did train him on,
And, his corruption being ta'en from us,
We as the spring of all shall pay for all.
Therefore, good cousin, let not Harry know
In any case the offer of the King.

sentences.speaker: VERNON
sentences.text: Deliver what you will; I’ll say ’tis so.
Enter HOTSPUR and DOUGLAS

sentences.speaker: VERNON
sentences.text: Here comes your cousin.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
My uncle is returned.
Deliver up my Lord of Westmoreland.—
Uncle, what news?

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
My uncle is returned.
Deliver up my Lord of Westmoreland.—
Uncle, what news?

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
My uncle is returned.
Deliver up my Lord of Westmoreland.—
Uncle, what news?

sentences.speaker: WORCESTER
sentences.text: The King will bid you battle presently.

sentences.speaker: DOUGLAS
sentences.text: Defy him by the Lord of Westmoreland.

sentences.speaker: DOUGLAS
sentences.text: Defy him by the Lord of Westmoreland.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text: Lord Douglas, go you and tell him so.

sentences.speaker: DOUGLAS
sentences.text: Marry, and shall, and very willingly.
Exit DOUGLAS

sentences.speaker: WORCESTER
sentences.text: There is no seeming mercy in the King.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text: Did you beg any? God forbid!

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text: Did you beg any? God forbid!

sentences.speaker: WORCESTER
sentences.text:
I told him gently of our grievances,
Of his oath-breaking, which he mended thus
By now forswearing that he is forsworn.
He calls us “rebels,” “traitors,” and will scourge
With haughty arms this hateful name in us.

Enter DOUGLAS

sentences.speaker: DOUGLAS
sentences.text:
Arm, gentlemen; to arms. For I have thrown
A brave defiance in King Henry’s teeth,
And Westmoreland, that was engaged, did bear it,
Which cannot choose but bring him quickly on.

sentences.speaker: WORCESTER
sentences.text:
The Prince of Wales stepped forth before the King,
And, nephew, challenged you to single fight.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
O, would the quarrel lay upon our heads,
And that no man might draw short breath today
But I and Harry Monmouth! Tell me, tell me,
How showed his tasking? Seemed it in contempt?

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:

And that no man might draw short breath today
But I and Harry Monmouth! Tell me, tell me,
How showed his tasking? Seemed it in contempt?

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
O, would the quarrel lay upon our heads,
And that no man might draw short breath today
But I and Harry Monmouth! Tell me, tell me,
How showed his tasking? Seemed it in contempt?

sentences.speaker: VERNON
sentences.text:
No, by my soul. I never in my life
Did hear a challenge urged more modestly,
Unless a brother should a brother dare
To gentle exercise and proof of arms.
He gave you all the duties of a man,
Trimmed up your praises with a princely tongue,
Spoke your deservings like a chronicle,
Making you ever better than his praise
By still dispraising praise valued in you,
And, which became him like a prince indeed,
He made a blushing cital of himself,
And chid his truant youth with such a grace
As if he mastered there a double spirit
Of teaching and of learning instantly.
There did he pause: but let me tell the world:
If he outlive the envy of this day,
England did never owe so sweet a hope,
So much misconstrued in his wantonness.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
Cousin, I think thou art enamorèd
On his follies. Never did I hear
Of any Prince so wild a liberty.
But be he as he will, yet once ere night
I will embrace him with a soldier’s arm,
That he shall shrink under my courtesy.—
Arm, arm with speed, and, fellows, soldiers, friends,
Better consider what you have to do
Than I that have not well the gift of tongue
Can lift your blood up with persuasion.

Enter a MESSENGER

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: My lord, here are letters for you.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
I cannot read them now.—
O gentlemen, the time of life is short;
To spend that shortness basely were too long
If life did ride upon a dial’s point,
Still ending at the arrival of an hour.
An if we live, we live to tread on kings;
If die, brave death, when princes die with us.
Now, for our consciences, the arms are fair
When the intent of bearing them is just.

Enter another MESSENGER

sentences.speaker: SECOND MESSENGER
sentences.text: My lord, prepare. The King comes on apace.

sentences.speaker: SECOND MESSENGER
sentences.text: My lord, prepare. The King comes on apace.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
I thank him that he cuts me from my tale,
For I profess not talking. Only this:
Let each man do his best. And here draw I a sword,
Whose temper I intend to stain
With the best blood that I can meet withal
In the adventure of this perilous day.
Now, Esperance! Percy! And set on.
Sound all the lofty instruments of war,
And by that music let us all embrace,
For, heaven to earth, some of us never shall
A second time do such a courtesy.

The KING enters with his power and they cross the stage. Alarum to the battle.
The KING enters with his power and they cross the stage. Alarum to the battle.
Then enter DOUGLAS and Sir Walter BLUNT, disguised as the KING

sentences.speaker: BLUNT
sentences.text:
What is thy name that in the battle thus
Thou crossest me? What honor dost thou seek
Upon my head?

sentences.speaker: BLUNT
sentences.text:
What is thy name that in the battle thus
Thou crossest me? What honor dost thou seek
Upon my head?

sentences.speaker: DOUGLAS
sentences.text:
Know then, my name is Douglas,
And I do haunt thee in the battle thus
Because some tell me that thou art a king.

sentences.speaker: DOUGLAS
sentences.text:
Know then, my name is Douglas,
And I do haunt thee in the battle thus
Because some tell me that thou art a king.

sentences.speaker: BLUNT
sentences.text: They tell thee true.

sentences.speaker: DOUGLAS
sentences.text:
The Lord of Stafford dear today hath bought
Thy likeness, for instead of thee, King Harry,
This sword hath ended him. So shall it thee,
Unless thou yield thee as my prisoner.

sentences.speaker: DOUGLAS
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: BLUNT
sentences.text:
I was not born a yielder, thou proud Scot,
And thou shalt find a king that will revenge
Lord Stafford’s death.

sentences.speaker: BLUNT
sentences.text:

They fight. DOUGLAS kills BLUNT. Enter HOTSPUR

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR

O Douglas, hadst thou fought at Holmedon thus,
I never had triumphed upon a Scot.

sentences.speaker: DOUGLAS
sentences.text: All’s done, all’s won; here breathless lies the King.

sentences.speaker: DOUGLAS

sentences.speaker: DOUGLAS
sentences.text: All’s done, all’s won; here breathless lies the King.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text: Where?

sentences.speaker: DOUGLAS
sentences.text: Here.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
This, Douglas? No, I know this face full well.
A gallant knight he was; his name was Blunt,
Semblably furnished like the King himself.

sentences.speaker: DOUGLAS
sentences.text:
[To BLUNT] A fool go with thy soul whither it goes!
A borrowed title hast thou bought too dear.
Why didst thou tell me that thou wert a king?

sentences.speaker: DOUGLAS
sentences.text:
Now, by my sword, I will kill all his coats.
I’ll murder all his wardrobe, piece by piece,
Until I meet the King.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR

Up and away!
Our soldiers stand full fairly for the day.

Alarum. Enter FALSTAFF alone.
Alarum. Enter FALSTAFF alone.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Though I could ’scape shot-free at London, I fear the
shot here. Here’s no scoring but upon the pate.— Soft,
who are you? Sir Walter Blunt. There’s honor for you.
Here’s no vanity. I am as hot as molten lead, and as
heavy too. God keep lead out of me; I need no more
weight than mine own bowels. I have led my ragamuffins
where they are peppered. There’s not three of my hundred
and fifty left alive, and they are for the town’s end,
to beg during life. But who comes here?

Enter PRINCE HENRY

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
What, stand’st thou idle here? Lend me thy sword.
Many a nobleman lies stark and stiff
Under the hoofs of vaunting enemies,
Whose deaths are yet unrevenged. I prithee,
Lend me thy sword.

O Hal, I prithee, give me leave to breathe awhile. Turk
Gregory never did such deeds in arms as I have done
this day. I have paid Percy; I have made him sure.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Well, if Percy be alive, I’ll pierce him. If he do come
in my way, so; if he do not, if I come in his
willingly, let him make a carbonado of me. I like not
such grinning honor as Sir Walter hath. Give me life,
which if I can save, so: if not, honor comes unlooked
for, and there’s an end.

such grinning honor as Sir Walter hath. Give me life,
which if I can save, so: if not, honor comes unlooked
for, and there’s an end.
Exit
Alarum. Excursions. Enter the KING, PRINCE HENRY, Lord John of LANCASTER, and the Earl of WESTMORELAND

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
I prithee, Harry, withdraw thyself. Thou bleedest too
much.
Lord John of Lancaster, go you with him.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
I prithee, Harry, withdraw thyself. Thou bleedest too
much.
Lord John of Lancaster, go you with him.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
I prithee, Harry, withdraw thyself. Thou bleedest too
much.
Lord John of Lancaster, go you with him.

sentences.speaker: LANCASTER
sentences.text: Not I, my lord, unless I did bleed too.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
I beseech your Majesty, make up,
Lest your retirement do amaze your friends.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
I beseech your Majesty, make up,
Lest your retirement do amaze your friends.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
I will do so.—My Lord of Westmoreland,
Lead him to his tent.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
I will do so.—My Lord of Westmoreland,
Lead him to his tent.

sentences.speaker: WESTMORELAND
sentences.text: Come, my lord, I’ll lead you to your tent.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Lead me, my lord? I do not need your help,
And God forbid a shallow scratch should drive
The Prince of Wales from such a field as this,
Where stained nobility lies trodden on,
And rebels' arms triumph in massacres.

sentences.text:

Our duty this way lies. For God’s sake, come.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
By God, thou hast deceived me, Lancaster.
I did not think thee lord of such a spirit.
Before, I loved thee as a brother, John,
But now, I do respect thee as my soul.

sentences.text:
I saw him hold Lord Percy at the point
With lustier maintenance than I did look for
Of such an ungrown warrior.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: O, this boy lends mettle to us all.
Exit
Enter DOUGLAS

sentences.speaker: DOUGLAS
sentences.text:
Another king! they grow like Hydra’s heads.—
I am the Douglas, fatal to all those
That wear those colors on them. What art thou
That counterfeit’st the person of a king?

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
The King himself, who, Douglas, grieves at heart,
So many of his shadows thou hast met
And not the very king. I have two boys
Seek Percy and thyself about the field,
But, seeing thou fall’st on me so luckily,
I will assay thee. And defend thyself.

sentences.text:
I fear thou art another counterfeit,
And yet, in faith, thou bear’st thee like a king.
But mine I am sure thou art, whoe'er thou be,
And thus I win thee.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Hold up thy head, vile Scot, or thou art like
Never to hold it up again. The spirits
Of valiant Shirley, Stafford, Blunt are in my arms.
It is the Prince of Wales that threatens thee,
Who never promiseth but he means to pay.

Cheerly, my lord. How fares your Grace?
Sir Nicholas Gawsey hath for succor sent,
And so hath Clifton. I’ll to Clifton straight.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
Stay, and breathe awhile.
Thou hast redeemed thy lost opinion
And showed thou mak’st some tender of my life
In this fair rescue thou hast brought to me.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
Stay, and breathe awhile.
Thou hast redeemed thy lost opinion
And showed thou mak’st some tender of my life
In this fair rescue thou hast brought to me.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
O God, they did me too much injury
That ever said I hearkened for your death.
If it were so, I might have let alone
The insulting hand of Douglas over you,
Which would have been as speedy in your end
As all the poisonous potions in the world,
And saved the treacherous labor of your son.

Enter HOTSPUR

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text: If I mistake not, thou art Harry Monmouth.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Thou speak’st as if I would deny my name.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text: My name is Harry Percy.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Why, then I see
A very valiant rebel of the name.
I am the Prince of Wales; and think not, Percy,
To share with me in glory any more.
Two stars keep not their motion in one sphere,
Nor can one England brook a double reign,
Of Harry Percy and the Prince of Wales.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
Nor shall it, Harry, for the hour is come
To end the one of us, and would to God
Thy name in arms were now as great as mine.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:

To end the one of us, and would to God

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
I’ll make it greater ere I part from thee,
And all the budding honors on thy crest
I’ll crop, to make a garland for my head.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
I’ll make it greater ere I part from thee,
And all the budding honors on thy crest
I’ll crop, to make a garland for my head.

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text: I can no longer brook thy vanities.
They fight

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Well said, Hal! To it Hal! Nay, you shall find no boys'
play here, I can tell you.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text: PRINCE HENRY killeth HOTSPUR

sentences.speaker: HOTSPUR
sentences.text:
O Harry, thou hast robbed me of my youth.
I better brook the loss of brittle life
Than those proud titles thou hast won of me.
They wound my thoughts worse than thy sword my flesh.
But thoughts, the slave of life, and life, time’s fool,
And time, that takes survey of all the world,
Must have a stop. O, I could prophesy,
But that the earthy and cold hand of death
Lies on my tongue. No, Percy, thou art dust,
And food for— (he dies)

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
For worms, brave Percy. Fare thee well, great heart.
Ill-weaved ambition, how much art thou shrunk!
When that this body did contain a spirit,
A kingdom for it was too small a bound,
But now two paces of the vilest earth
Is room enough. This earth that bears thee dead
Bears not alive so stout a gentleman.
If thou wert sensible of courtesy,
I should not make so dear a show of zeal.
But let my favors hide thy mangled face;
And even in thy behalf I’ll thank myself
For doing these fair rites of tenderness.
Adieu, and take thy praise with thee to heaven.
Thy ignominy sleep with thee in the grave,
But not remembered in thy epitaph.

Adieu, and take thy praise with thee to heaven.
Thy ignominy sleep with thee in the grave,
But not remembered in thy epitaph.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
For worms, brave Percy. Fare thee well, great heart.
Ill-weaved ambition, how much art thou shrunk!
When that this body did contain a spirit,
A kingdom for it was too small a bound,
But now two paces of the vilest earth
Is room enough. This earth that bears thee dead
Bears not alive so stout a gentleman.
If thou wert sensible of courtesy,
I should not make so dear a show of zeal.
But let my favors hide thy mangled face;
And even in thy behalf I’ll thank myself
For doing these fair rites of tenderness.
Adieu, and take thy praise with thee to heaven.
Thy ignominy sleep with thee in the grave,
But not remembered in thy epitaph.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: FALSTAFF riseth up

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Emboweled? If thou embowel me today, I’ll give you
leave to powder me and eat me too tomorrow. 'Sblood,
’twas time to counterfeit, or that hot termagant Scot
had paid me scot and lot too. Counterfeit? I lie. I am
no counterfeit. To die is to be a counterfeit, for he is
but the counterfeit of a man who hath not the life of a
man; but to counterfeit dying when a man thereby liveth
is to be no counterfeit, but the true and perfect image
of life indeed. The better part of valor is discretion,
in the which better part I have saved my life. Zounds,
I am afraid of this gunpowder Percy, though he be dead.
How if he should counterfeit too and rise? By my faith,
I am afraid he would prove the better counterfeit.
Therefore I’ll make him sure, yea, and I’ll swear I
killed him. Why may not he rise as well as I? Nothing
confutes me but eyes, and nobody sees me. Therefore,
sirrah, (stabs the body) with a new wound in your thigh,
come you along with me. (he takes up HOTSPUR on his
back)

Enter PRINCE HENRY and Lord John of LANCASTER

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Come, brother John. Full bravely hast thou fleshed
Thy maiden sword.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Come, brother John. Full bravely hast thou fleshed
Thy maiden sword.

sentences.speaker: LANCASTER
sentences.text:
But soft, whom have we here?
Did you not tell me this fat man was dead?

sentences.speaker: LANCASTER
sentences.text:
But soft, whom have we here?
Did you not tell me this fat man was dead?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
I did; I saw him dead,
Breathless and bleeding on the ground.— Art thou alive?
Or is it fantasy that plays upon our eyesight?
I prithee, speak. We will not trust our eyes
Without our ears. Thou art not what thou seem’st.

No, that’s certain. I am not a double man. But if I be
not Jack Falstaff, then am I a jack. There is Percy. If
your father will do me any honor, so; if not, let him
kill the next Percy himself. I look to be either earl or
duke, I can assure you.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: Why, Percy I killed myself, and saw thee dead.

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
Didst thou? Lord, Lord, how this world is given to
lying. I grant you, Iwas down and out of breath, and so
was he, but we rose both at an instant and fought a long
hour by Shrewsbury clock. If I may be believed, so; if
not, let them that should reward valor bear the sin upon
their own heads. I’ll take it upon my death, I gave him
this wound in the thigh. If the man were alive and
would deny it, zounds, I would make him eat a piece of
my sword.

sentences.speaker: LANCASTER
sentences.text: This is the strangest tale that ever I heard.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
This is the strangest fellow, brother John.—
Come, bring your luggage nobly on your back.
For my part, if a lie may do thee grace,
I’ll gild it with the happiest terms I have.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
This is the strangest fellow, brother John.—
Come, bring your luggage nobly on your back.
For my part, if a lie may do thee grace,
I’ll gild it with the happiest terms I have.
A retreat is sounded

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
The trumpet sounds retreat; the day is ours.
Come, brother, let us to the highest of the field
To see what friends are living, who are dead.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
The trumpet sounds retreat; the day is ours.
Come, brother, let us to the highest of the field
To see what friends are living, who are dead.
Exeunt PRINCE HENRY and Lord John of LANCASTER

sentences.speaker: FALSTAFF
sentences.text:
I’ll follow, as they say, for reward. He that rewards
me, God reward him. If I do grow great, I’ll grow less,
for I’ll purge and leave sack and live cleanly as a
nobleman should do.

The trumpets sound. Enter the KING, PRINCE HENRY, Lord John of LANCASTER, Earl of WESTMORELAND, with WORCESTER and VERNON prisoners
The trumpets sound. Enter the KING, PRINCE HENRY, Lord John of LANCASTER, Earl of WESTMORELAND, with WORCESTER and VERNON prisoners

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
Thus ever did rebellion find rebuke.—
Ill-spirited Worcester, did not we send grace,
Pardon, and terms of love to all of you?
And wouldst thou turn our offers contrary,
Misuse the tenor of thy kinsman’s trust?
Three knights upon our party slain today,
A noble earl, and many a creature else
Had been alive this hour,
If like a Christian thou hadst truly borne
Betwixt our armies true intelligence.

sentences.speaker: WORCESTER
sentences.text:
What I have done my safety urged me to.
And I embrace this fortune patiently,
Since not to be avoided it falls on me.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
Bear Worcester to the death, and Vernon too.
Other offenders we will pause upon.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
Bear Worcester to the death, and Vernon too.
Other offenders we will pause upon.
Exeunt WORCESTER and VERNON under guard

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text: How goes the field?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
The noble Scot, Lord Douglas, when he saw
The fortune of the day quite turned from him,
The noble Percy slain, and all his men
Upon the foot of fear, fled with the rest,
And, falling from a hill, he was so bruised
That the pursuers took him. At my tent
The Douglas is, and I beseech your Grace
I may dispose of him.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text: With all my heart.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Then, brother John of Lancaster, to you
This honorable bounty shall belong.
Go to the Douglas, and deliver him
Up to his pleasure, ransomless and free.
His valor shown upon our crests today
Hath taught us how to cherish such high deeds,
Even in the bosom of our adversaries.

sentences.speaker: LANCASTER
sentences.text:
I thank your Grace for this high courtesy,
Which I shall give away immediately.

sentences.speaker: KING
sentences.text:
Then this remains, that we divide our power.
You, son John, and my cousin Westmoreland,
Towards York shall bend you with your dearest speed
To meet Northumberland and the prelate Scroop,
Who, as we hear, are busily in arms.
Myself and you, son Harry, will towards Wales
To fight with Glendower and the Earl of March.
Rebellion in this land shall lose his sway,
Meeting the check of such another day.
And since this business so fair is done,
Let us not leave till all our own be won.
